functions.method_signature: def load_json_file(file_path: str, encoding: str = "utf-8") -> dict

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the JSON file to load

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: Character encoding to use when reading the file. Defaults to 'utf-8'.

functions.what_it_does: Loads and parses a JSON file, returning the parsed data as a dictionary with error handling. Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Opens the file at file_path with the specified encoding using open() in read mode. Parses the JSON content using json.load() and returns the resulting dictionary. If FileNotFoundError is raised, re-raises it with a descriptive message including the file path. If json.JSONDecodeError is raised during parsing, re-raises it as a ValueError with a message including the file path and original error. This provides clear error messages for common JSON loading failures.

Loads locale mapping data from a JSON file defined in Constants.LOCALE_MAP_PATH. Simply calls load_json_file() with the path from Constants.LOCALE_MAP_PATH and returns the parsed dictionary. This provides centralized access to locale configuration data used throughout the framework for internationalization and localization.

Loads printer profile configuration data from a JSON file defined in Constants.PRINTER_PROFILES_PATH. Simply calls load_json_file() with the path from Constants.PRINTER_PROFILES_PATH and returns the parsed dictionary. This provides centralized access to printer profile data including SKUs, regions, and capabilities used for test data generation.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to retrieve address data for

functions.what_it_does: Loads address data for a specific country from the address data JSON file. First calls load_json_file() with Constants.ADDRESS_DATA_PATH to load the complete address data dictionary. Then uses dict.get() to retrieve and return the address data for the specified country key. Returns None if the country is not found in the data. This provides test data for address fields during enrollment and checkout flows.

functions.method_signature: def get_printer_details_for_country(profile_name: str, region: str, printer_profiles: dict, easy_enroll_flag: str = None, biz_model: str = "Flex") -> tuple

parameters.name: profile_name
parameters.description: Name of the printer profile to retrieve

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Region identifier to get SKUs for

parameters.name: printer_profiles
parameters.description: Dictionary of printer profile data loaded from JSON

parameters.name: easy_enroll_flag
parameters.description: Optional 'yes' or 'no' string to filter SKUs by easy_enroll capability

parameters.name: biz_model
parameters.description: Business model identifier. Defaults to 'Flex'.

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves printer details including SKU and supply variant for a specific profile, region, and optional easy enroll filter. First validates that profile_name exists in printer_profiles dictionary, raising ValueError if not found. Gets the SKUs dictionary for the profile and validates that region exists, raising ValueError if not found. Retrieves the list of SKUs for the region. If easy_enroll_flag is provided, converts it to boolean and filters the SKUs list to only include SKUs where the easy_enroll property matches the desired value, raising ValueError if no matching SKUs are found. Selects the first SKU from the filtered or unfiltered list. Extracts the sku and supplyvariant values from the selected SKU dictionary. Returns a tuple containing (profile_name, sku, supplyvariant, biz_model). This is used to generate printer configuration for test flows.

parameters.name: svg_xml
parameters.description: SVG XML content as string to extract animation duration from

parameters.name: fallback_duration_ms
parameters.description: Fallback duration in milliseconds if no duration is found. Defaults to 1000ms.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animation duration from SVG XML content by searching for SMIL or CSS animation duration attributes. First uses re.search() to look for SMIL dur attribute with pattern 'dur="([\d\.]+)(ms|s)"'. If found, extracts the numeric value and unit. Converts to milliseconds if unit is 'ms', or multiplies by 1000 if unit is 's', then returns as integer. If SMIL duration not found, searches for CSS animation-duration property with pattern 'animation-duration:\s*([\d\.]+)(ms|s)'. If found, performs the same conversion and returns. If neither pattern is found, returns the fallback_duration_ms value. This is used to determine appropriate frame capture timing for animated SVGs.

functions.method_signature: def extract_animated_svgs(page, min_size=5120, poll_interval=0.02, max_frames=100, upscale_factor=4, save_debug_frames=True, debug_frame_dir="./debug_svg_frames")

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to extract SVGs from

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum SVG size in bytes to consider. Defaults to 5120.

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between frame captures. Defaults to 0.02s (50fps).

parameters.name: max_frames
parameters.description: Maximum number of frames to capture. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: upscale_factor
parameters.description: Factor to upscale SVG dimensions for higher quality capture. Defaults to 4.

parameters.name: save_debug_frames
parameters.description: Whether to save individual debug frames. Defaults to True.

parameters.name: debug_frame_dir
parameters.description: Directory to save debug frames. Defaults to './debug_svg_frames'.

functions.what_it_does: Comprehensive function that detects, captures, and converts animated SVG elements on a page to GIF format with extensive animation detection logic. Queries all SVG elements on the page. For each SVG: checks visibility, extracts outer HTML, validates size against min_size threshold. Detects animation using multiple strategies: SMIL tags (animate, animateTransform), CSS animations and transitions via computed styles, JavaScript timers and requestAnimationFrame, script tags and event handlers. Logs detailed debug information about animation detection. Performs hard reset of animations by removing and re-adding elements to DOM. Triggers animations via hover and mouseenter events. Waits fixed initial delay. Captures multiple detect frames to identify pixel-level changes using PIL Image comparison and hash comparison. If animation detected, upscales SVG dimensions by upscale_factor. Captures frames at poll_interval timing, detecting loop points by comparing frame hashes to first frame. Calculates per-frame duration based on detected loop period. Restores original SVG dimensions. Converts captured frames to GIF using PIL with optimized palette, calculated duration, and disposal mode 2. Returns list of result dictionaries containing element handle, SVG XML, GIF bytes, frame count, resolution, animation type, and timing metadata. This is the most complex animation extraction function in the framework.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to extract GIFs from

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum GIF file size in bytes to consider. Defaults to 1024.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animated GIF images from a web page by finding img elements with .gif sources and downloading them. Queries all img elements with src attributes containing '.gif' using page.query_selector_all(). For each GIF element: checks if visible, gets the src attribute, validates it contains '.gif'. Makes an HTTP GET request to the GIF URL using page.context.request.get(). Checks response status is 200. Gets the response body as bytes. Validates the size is at least min_size bytes. Opens the GIF bytes with PIL Image and checks if it has is_animated attribute set to True and n_frames > 1 to confirm it's actually animated. If all checks pass, appends a result dictionary containing the element handle, src URL, bytes content, and size to the results list. Returns the list of extracted animated GIFs. Catches and logs exceptions for individual GIFs without stopping the entire extraction.

functions.method_signature: def extract_animated_visuals(page, selector=".stepsImage", min_size=1024, screenshot_dir=".", context_hint=None, poll_interval=0.03, max_frames=60, detect_loop=True, save_debug_frames=False)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object to extract animations from

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: CSS selector to find animation containers. Defaults to '.stepsImage'.

parameters.name: min_size
parameters.description: Minimum output file size in bytes. Defaults to 1024.

parameters.name: screenshot_dir
parameters.description: Directory to save output files. Defaults to current directory.

parameters.name: context_hint
parameters.description: Optional context name for filename generation

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between frame captures. Defaults to 0.03s.

parameters.name: max_frames
parameters.description: Maximum frames to capture. Defaults to 60.

parameters.name: detect_loop
parameters.description: Whether to detect animation loops. Defaults to True.

parameters.name: save_debug_frames
parameters.description: Whether to save individual frame PNGs. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts animated visual elements (SVG, Canvas, Lottie, CSS sprites) from page elements matching a selector and converts them to GIF or PNG. Queries all elements matching the selector. For each element: checks visibility, gets inner HTML and tag name. Detects animation type by checking for SVG tags, canvas elements, 'lottie' in HTML, or CSS background-image sprites. Generates filename using context_hint and animation type. Captures frames in a loop up to max_frames: forces repaint by setting random outline style, scrolls element into view, takes screenshot, converts to PIL Image, computes SHA-256 hash of image bytes, stores frame and hash. Optionally saves debug frame PNGs if save_debug_frames is True. Detects loop by comparing current frame hash to first frame hash after frame 3. Determines if animated by checking for multiple unique frame hashes. If animated with 2+ frames: calculates duration based on detected loop period or poll_interval, converts frames to palette mode, saves as optimized GIF with calculated duration and loop=0. If static: saves first frame as PNG. Validates output size against min_size and deletes if too small. Returns list of result dictionaries with element, animation_type, screenshot_path, is_animated flag, frames_captured count, duration_per_frame_ms, size, and tag. This provides flexible animation extraction for various animation technologies.

parameters.name: exclude_str
parameters.description: String representation of exclusion regions, typically from Excel cell

functions.what_it_does: Parses exclusion region string into a list of tuples representing rectangular areas to exclude from image comparison. Returns empty list if exclude_str is empty or None. Uses ast.literal_eval() to safely parse the string as a Python literal (list or tuple). If the result is a single tuple (x, y, width, height), wraps it in a list. Returns the list of exclusion tuples. If parsing fails due to invalid syntax, catches the exception and returns empty list. This allows validation to ignore specific screen regions that are expected to differ (timestamps, dynamic content, etc.).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of pixel to check

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of pixel to check

parameters.name: exclusions
parameters.description: List of exclusion region tuples (x, y, width, height)

functions.what_it_does: Checks if a pixel coordinate falls within any of the defined exclusion regions. Iterates through each exclusion tuple in the exclusions list. For each exclusion, unpacks the four values as ex_x, ex_y, ex_w, ex_h representing the top-left corner and dimensions of the exclusion rectangle. Checks if the provided x coordinate is within the range [ex_x, ex_x + ex_w) and the y coordinate is within [ex_y, ex_y + ex_h). Returns True immediately if the pixel is inside any exclusion region. Returns False if the pixel is not inside any exclusion region after checking all. This is used during pixel-by-pixel image comparison to skip pixels in excluded areas.

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: List of (x, y) tuples representing failed pixel coordinates

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Tuple of (width, height) representing image dimensions

functions.what_it_does: Converts a list of failed pixel coordinates into bounding box rectangles using flood fill algorithm. Returns empty list if no pixels provided. Unpacks width and height from size tuple. Creates a binary mask image with all pixels set to 0. Sets mask pixels to 255 for each failed pixel coordinate. Creates a visited set to track processed pixels. Defines inner flood_fill function that performs iterative flood fill using a stack: starts from a seed pixel, explores all connected pixels with value 255, tracks min/max x and y coordinates, returns bounding box tuple (xmin, ymin, width, height). Iterates through all pixels in the mask. For each unvisited pixel with value 255, calls flood_fill to get its bounding box. Only adds boxes where both width and height are >= MIN_TOLERANCE_PIXELS (1). Returns list of bounding box tuples. This generates suggested exclusion regions for visual differences by grouping adjacent failed pixels into rectangular regions.

parameters.name: img1_path
parameters.description: Path to baseline image file

parameters.name: img2_path
parameters.description: Path to validation image file

parameters.name: exclusions
parameters.description: List of exclusion region tuples

parameters.name: color_tolerance
parameters.description: Maximum allowed color difference per pixel. Defaults to COLOR_TOLERANCE constant (35).

parameters.name: original_exclude_str
parameters.description: Original exclusion string from Excel. If '[]', returns pass immediately.

functions.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel comparison of two images with exclusion region support and generates suggested exclusion boxes for differences. Returns (False, None, [], []) if either image file does not exist. If original_exclude_str is '[]', returns (True, None, [], []) immediately treating empty exclusion list as pass. Opens both images and converts to RGB mode. Gets dimensions of both images and determines the maximum width and height. Creates two new white base images of the maximum dimensions and pastes the original images at (0,0) to handle size mismatches. Initializes two lists: failed_pixels for all differences and failed_pixels_outside_exclusions for differences not in exclusion regions. Iterates through every pixel coordinate in the combined image dimensions. For each pixel, gets RGB values from both images, calculates Euclidean distance between colors, and if distance exceeds color_tolerance, adds to failed_pixels list and checks if pixel is outside exclusions using is_in_exclusion() - if outside, adds to failed_pixels_outside_exclusions. After checking all pixels, calls get_bounding_boxes() on failed_pixels_outside_exclusions to generate suggested exclusion regions. Returns (True, None, [], failed_pixels) if no boxes generated (pass). Returns (False, None, boxes, failed_pixels_outside_exclusions) if boxes exist (fail). This is the core image comparison engine with smart exclusion handling.

parameters.name: img2_path
parameters.description: Path to validation image to overlay failures on

parameters.name: failed_pixels_outside_exclusions
parameters.description: List of (x, y) tuples for pixels that failed outside exclusions

parameters.name: out_path
parameters.description: Output path for the overlaid diff image

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Alpha transparency value for red overlay (0-255). Defaults to 120.

functions.what_it_does: Creates a visual diff image by overlaying red semi-transparent pixels on failed areas of the validation image. Opens the validation image at img2_path and converts to RGBA mode to support transparency. Creates a new transparent overlay image of the same size with all pixels set to (255, 0, 0, 0) - red with full transparency. Gets the pixel data accessor for the overlay. Unpacks width and height from image size. Iterates through each failed pixel coordinate in failed_pixels_outside_exclusions. For each coordinate, checks if it's within image bounds, and if so, sets the overlay pixel to (255, 0, 0, alpha) - red with specified transparency. Uses Image.alpha_composite() to blend the validation image with the red overlay, creating a composite image where failed pixels appear as semi-transparent red. Saves the composite image to out_path. Returns True on success. This creates intuitive visual diff images showing exactly where validation failed.

functions.method_signature: def compare_gif_files(gif_path_1: str, gif_path_2: str) -> bool

parameters.name: gif_path_1
parameters.description: Path to first GIF file

parameters.name: gif_path_2
parameters.description: Path to second GIF file

functions.docstring: Compare two GIF files byte-wise. Returns True if identical.
functions.what_it_does: Performs byte-by-byte comparison of two GIF files to determine if they are identical. First checks if both files exist using os.path.exists(). If either file is missing, logs a warning with both file paths and returns False. Opens both files in binary read mode using context managers. Reads the entire content of both files using f.read() and compares them with == operator. Returns True if the byte content is identical, False otherwise. This is used for validating animated GIF assets during validation runs.

functions.method_signature: def compare_svg_xml(svg_xml_1: str, svg_xml_2: str) -> bool

parameters.name: svg_xml_1
parameters.description: First SVG XML content as string

parameters.name: svg_xml_2
parameters.description: Second SVG XML content as string

functions.docstring:
Robust SVG comparison: ignores whitespace, attribute order, float precision, and comment nodes.
Handles: attribute sorting, rounds floats, strips comments and normalizes text/tail.
functions.what_it_does: Performs robust semantic comparison of two SVG XML strings with normalization to ignore insignificant differences. Defines inner normalize_float() function that rounds float values to 3 decimals and handles space/comma-separated float lists in path data. Defines inner clean_element() function that recursively processes XML elements: sorts attributes alphabetically, normalizes float values in attributes, strips text and tail whitespace, removes comment nodes, sorts child elements by tag+attributes+text for deterministic order. Defines inner get_root() function that parses XML string using ET.fromstring() with error handling. Parses both SVG strings to get root elements. If either parse fails, falls back to whitespace-insensitive string comparison using re.sub() to remove all whitespace. If both parse successfully, applies clean_element() to both roots to normalize them. Serializes both normalized trees back to strings using ET.tostring(). Compares the normalized strings and returns True if identical, False otherwise. This handles common SVG differences like attribute order, float precision, whitespace, and comments that don't affect visual rendering.

functions.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)

parameters.name: flow_name
parameters.description: Name of the test flow being validated

parameters.name: context_name
parameters.description: Name of the context/checkpoint being validated

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object of the live page to validate

parameters.name: excel_path
parameters.description: Path to baseline Excel file with scraped data

parameters.name: output_folder
parameters.description: Directory to save validation results

parameters.name: validation_workbook_path
parameters.description: Optional path to validation results workbook. Auto-generated if None.

parameters.name: screenshot_only
parameters.description: If True, only captures full-page screenshot without element validation

parameters.name: sub_context
parameters.description: Optional sub-context name for granular checkpoints

parameters.name: lang_code
parameters.description: Language code for filename generation

parameters.name: animations
parameters.description: If True, validates animated SVG and GIF assets

parameters.name: target_dir
parameters.description: Target platform directory name

functions.what_it_does: Core validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Creates output directories for validation screenshots, failure screenshots, and animations. Constructs subcontext suffix for filenames. If screenshot_only mode: captures full-page screenshot with retry logic, loads baseline Excel to find matching full-page baseline, performs image comparison with exclusions, generates validation report row with pass/fail status and suggested exclusion regions if failed, overlays red highlighting on failures, writes report to Excel, and returns early. For full validation: loads baseline workbook and finds the context sheet, extracts header row, filters baseline data rows matching context and sub_context. If animations flag is True: extracts all animated SVGs from page using extract_animated_svgs() and saves them to validation_animations folder. Queries all visible elements on live page with text content and bounding boxes, storing them in live_elements list. Iterates through each baseline row: handles animation type rows by extracting and comparing GIF or SVG animations byte-wise or semantically, handles fullpage type rows by capturing and comparing full-page screenshots with exclusions, handles regular element rows by finding matching live elements by text and tag, comparing text and href, capturing element screenshots, comparing with baseline screenshots using exclusions, generating suggested exclusion boxes for failures, and overlaying red highlighting on diff images. Builds comprehensive validation report rows with all comparison results. Writes all results to Excel with automatic highlighting using write_report_excel_context(). Logs completion. This is the most complex validation function implementing the entire validation pipeline with support for multiple validation types and detailed reporting.

functions.method_signature: def select_cancellation_feedback_option(page, feedback_option="I do not print enough", submit: bool = True)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: feedback_option
parameters.description: Feedback reason to select. Options: 'I do not print enough', 'The service is too expensive', 'I have not received my shipment', 'I have replaced my printer', 'Wi-Fi issues with my printer', 'Other'. Defaults to 'I do not print enough'.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: Whether to submit the feedback or return to account. Defaults to True.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a cancellation feedback option from the ink subscription cancellation feedback form and optionally submits it. Creates CancellationPage instance. Clicks the continue button with 120 second timeout to proceed to feedback form. Defines option_map dictionary mapping feedback option strings to their corresponding radio button element selectors. Looks up the selector for the provided feedback_option in the map, raising ValueError if not found. Clicks the radio button using page.click() with force=True. If feedback_option is 'Other', fills the feedback text box with 'Other Reason'. If submit parameter is True, clicks the submit feedback button; otherwise clicks the return to account button. This allows tests to select different cancellation reasons and control whether to submit or cancel.

functions.method_signature: def select_paper_cancellation_feedback_option(page, feedback_option="I'm worried I'm going to run out of paper", submit: bool = True)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: feedback_option
parameters.default_value: "I'm worried I'm going to run out of paper"
parameters.description: Paper cancellation feedback reason. Multiple options available including replenishment anxiety, leakage, didn't print enough, ran out, overages, temporary, stockpiling, expensive, damaged, didn't receive, moved, quality, other.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: Whether to submit or return to account. Defaults to True.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a cancellation feedback option specifically for paper subscription cancellation. Creates CancellationPage instance and clicks continue button with 120 second timeout. Defines comprehensive option_map with 13 paper-specific cancellation reasons mapped to their radio button selectors including replenishment anxiety, leakage, didn't print enough, ran out of paper, paper overages, temporary cancellation, stockpiling, too expensive, damaged paper, didn't receive shipment, moved to non-service area, paper quality issues, and other. Looks up selector for feedback_option, raising ValueError with descriptive message if not found. Clicks the radio button with force=True. If 'Other' is selected, fills feedback text box with 'Other Reason'. If submit is True, clicks submit button; otherwise clicks return to account button. This handles the paper-specific cancellation flow which has different feedback options than ink cancellation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: section_title
parameters.description: Title of the retention section to verify. Options: 'Have your printing needs changed?', 'Pause your subscription for up to 6 months', 'Did you get a new printer?', 'Have questions or need help?', 'Only pay when you need to print'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific retention option section is displayed correctly on the cancellation page with image, text, and link. Creates CancellationPage instance. Defines link_selectors dictionary mapping section titles to their corresponding link element selectors. Looks up selector for section_title, raising ValueError if not found. Locates the retention option container by finding the link's parent's parent element using XPath navigation. Within the container, locates the image, text paragraph (last p element), and button/link. Uses Playwright expect() to verify the image is visible, the text is visible, the text content matches the section_title, and the link is visible. This ensures retention options are properly displayed to encourage users not to cancel.
functions.usage_example: CancellationPlanHelper.verify_bottom_section(page, 'Pause your subscription for up to 6 months')

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the cancellation timeline page displays all required elements after cancellation is submitted. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. Uses Playwright expect() with 90 second timeout to verify header_icon is visible. Verifies header_title, header_subtitle, restore_subscription link, and whats_happens_next section are all visible. The timeline_img verification is commented out. This ensures the cancellation confirmation page loaded correctly with all key elements present.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the cancellation timeline steps display correct dates and content. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. Gets today's date and formats as 'MMM DD, YYYY'. Extracts the last day date from step 1 text using regex to find date pattern. Parses the last day date and calculates final bill date as last_day + 1 day. Verifies first step icon and text are visible and contain current date with 'Cancellation submitted'. Verifies second step icon and text are visible and contain last day date with 'Last day to print with Instant Ink cartridges'. For third step, uses regex pattern to match date range format 'MMM DD-DD, YYYY' for cartridge deactivation date, allowing for date range flexibility. Verifies fourth and fifth step icons and text are visible. This ensures the timeline accurately reflects the cancellation schedule with proper date calculations.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the 'Change Your Mind' retention section displays keep subscription and transfer subscription options correctly. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. For keep subscription section: verifies keep_subscription_as_it_was element is visible with 90 second timeout, locates the retention option text and link using specific XPath selectors, verifies text is visible with 90 second timeout, asserts text content is 'Restore subscription?' after stripping whitespace, verifies link is visible with 90 second timeout. For transfer subscription section: locates text and link using XPath selectors, verifies text is visible with 90 second timeout, asserts text content is 'Did you get a new printer?' after stripping whitespace, verifies link is visible with 90 second timeout. This ensures both retention options are properly displayed with correct text and clickable links.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: option
parameters.description: Button option to validate. Options: 'Shop HP Ink', 'Preview Upcoming Bill', 'Request Free Recycling Materials', 'Close'

functions.what_it_does: Validates that specific buttons on the cancellation timeline page work correctly by clicking them and verifying expected behavior. Creates CancellationTimelinePage instance. If option is 'Shop HP Ink': verifies shop_hp_button is visible, clicks it expecting a popup, waits for new page, verifies URL contains '/shop/cat', closes new page. If option is 'Preview Upcoming Bill': verifies preview_upcoming_button is visible, clicks expecting popup, waits for new page, closes it. If option is 'Request Free Recycling Materials': verifies request_recycling_button is visible, clicks expecting popup, waits for new page, verifies URL contains '/uat-inktoner-recycle', closes new page. If option is 'Close': verifies close button is visible, clicks it, verifies it becomes not visible. This tests the functionality of action buttons on the cancellation timeline.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the initial state of the cancellation feedback form with all elements in their default state. Creates CancellationPage instance. Verifies cancellation_feedback_title and cancellation_feedback_subtitle are visible. Asserts that all six radio buttons (do not print enough, wifi issues, too expensive, didn't receive shipment, replaced printer, other) are not checked using page.is_checked(). Verifies feedback_text_box is visible and has disabled attribute set (not None). Verifies submit_feedback_button is visible and has disabled attribute set. Verifies return_to_account_button is visible and does not have disabled attribute (is None). This ensures the form loads in the correct initial state with no options selected and submit button disabled until a selection is made.

Defines radio_selectors list with all six radio button element selectors. Iterates through each radio button by index. For each radio: locates the parent element using '..' XPath and clicks it to select the radio. Asserts the radio is checked. Then iterates through all other radios and asserts they are not checked. This ensures proper radio button group behavior where selecting one automatically deselects others.

Defines paper_radio_selectors list with all 13 paper cancellation radio button selectors including replenishment anxiety, leakage, didn't print enough, ran out, overages, temporary, stockpiling, expensive, damaged, didn't receive, moved, quality, and other. Iterates through each radio by index. For each radio: locates parent element and clicks to select. Asserts the radio is checked. Iterates through all other radios and asserts they are not checked. This ensures proper radio button group behavior for the paper-specific cancellation feedback form.

Verifies cancellation_animation is visible with 30 second timeout. Waits for continue_button to become visible with 90 second timeout indicating timeline page loaded. Records end_time using datetime.now() and prints it. Calculates duration in seconds by subtracting start_time from end_time. Prints the duration. Asserts duration is >= 4 seconds with descriptive error message. Waits for continue_button again. This ensures the loading animation displays long enough for users to see it.

Checks if 'login' is in the current page URL. If login page is detected and tenant_email is not provided, raises ValueError indicating email is required. If tenant_email is provided, calls HPIDHelper.sign_in() to authenticate. Creates DashboardSideMenuPage instance and gets the visible Instant Ink menu link element. Defines elements list containing lambda functions that return modal elements: special_savings_modal_close, flip_modal_not_now_button, continue_setting_preferences, accept_all_preferences, no_paper_offer_modal, skip_tour, and special_savings_modal_close again. If stack is 'pie' or 'stage', appends another special_savings_modal_close to the list. Performs up to 6 attempts to dismiss modals. In each attempt, iterates through remaining elements, tries to get each element and check if visible, clicks if visible and removes from list, catches exceptions silently. Sleeps 2 seconds between attempts. This ensures a clean dashboard state by dismissing all first-time user experience modals.

functions.what_it_does: Routes to appropriate payment method handler based on payment_method parameter or GlobalState. If payment_method is None, gets it from GlobalState.payment_method. Checks if payment_method contains 'paypal' and calls add_paypal_billing() if true, then returns. Checks if payment_method equals 'google_pay' and calls add_google_pay_billing() if true, then returns. Checks if payment_method equals 'apple_pay' and calls add_apple_pay_billing() if true, then returns. Checks if payment_method contains 'direct_debit' and calls add_direct_debit_billing() if true, then returns. If none of the above, gets card_payment_gateway from GlobalState. If gateway is 'PGS', calls add_pgs_billing(). If gateway is '2CO', calls add_2co_billing(). Otherwise raises ValueError with unsupported payment provider message. This is the main entry point for adding any payment method, routing to specialized handlers.

Gets payment data using common.get_payment_method_data() with card_type. Logs the card type being used. If country code is 'IT', fills tax_id_input with Italian tax ID. Checks if billing_continue_button is enabled and clicks it. Waits for PGS iframe to be visible with 120 second timeout. Gets frame locator for the iframe. Fills card number input with credit card number from payment data. Selects expiration month and year from dropdowns. Types CVV into cvv_input. Checks if billing_next_button exists in frame and clicks it if present. Otherwise clicks sca_save_button for Strong Customer Authentication. Handles 3DS challenge: waits for either redirectTo3ds1Frame or iframe_2fa to appear with 5 second timeout, gets the appropriate challenge frame locator, waits for authentication_result_2fa dropdown, selects authentication result from payment data (defaults to 'AUTHENTICATED'), waits for and clicks the submit button. Waits for update_shipping_billing_message to appear with 60 second timeout confirming success. This handles the complete PGS payment flow including 3DS2 authentication.

Checks billing_continue_button is enabled and clicks it. Waits for 2CO iframe to be visible with 120 second timeout. Waits for and clicks continue_to_billing button in iframe with 60 second timeout. Waits for and clicks continue_to_payment button in iframe with 60 second timeout. Waits for card_name_2co field to be attached with 3 second timeout. Checks if card_name_2co is visible and fills with 'John Doe' if present. Otherwise waits for first_name_2co, types 'John', presses Tab, waits for last_name_2co, types 'Doe'. Presses Tab, types credit card number into card_number_2co. Presses Tab, types expiration date as MM/YY format into exp_date_2co. Presses Tab, types CVV into cvv_2co. Waits for place_order_button to be visible with 60 second timeout and clicks it. Waits for update_shipping_billing_message to appear with 60 second timeout. Logs success. functions.method_signature: def sees_email_with_subject(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: The email address to check for received emails

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The email subject line to search for

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for the email to arrive. Defaults to 240 seconds (4 minutes).

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between each check for the email. Defaults to 10 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that an email with a specific subject has been received by the specified email address. This is a wrapper method that internally calls email_by_subject() to perform the actual email retrieval. The method polls the Gmail API at regular intervals (poll_interval) until either the email is found or the timeout period expires. If the email is found, the method completes successfully. If the timeout is reached without finding the email, the underlying email_by_subject() method will raise a RuntimeError. This method is typically used in test assertions to verify that expected emails were sent and received.
functions.usage_example: EmailHelper.sees_email_with_subject('testuser@example.com', 'Welcome to HP Instant Ink', timeout=300, poll_interval=15)

functions.method_signature: def email_by_subject(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: The email address to check for received emails

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The email subject line to search for

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for the email to arrive. Defaults to 240 seconds.

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between each check for the email. Defaults to 10 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an email message from Gmail API by searching for a specific subject line sent to a specific email address. First authenticates with Gmail using common.authenticate_gmail() to get the service object. Constructs a search query in the format 'to:{email} subject:"{subject}"' and repeatedly polls the Gmail API using service.users().messages().list() until either a matching email is found or the timeout expires. When a matching email is found, retrieves the full message details using service.users().messages().get() with the message ID and returns the complete message object. If no email is found within the timeout period, raises a RuntimeError with a descriptive message indicating the email was not received. Uses time.sleep(poll_interval) between polling attempts to avoid excessive API calls.

functions.method_signature: def extract_email_details(tenant_email: str, subject: str, timeout: int = 240, poll_interval: int = 10)

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: The email address to check for received emails

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The email subject line to search for

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for the email. Defaults to 240 seconds.

parameters.name: poll_interval
parameters.description: Time in seconds between polling attempts. Defaults to 10 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Extracts detailed information from an email message including subject, pre-header text, and HTML body content. First calls email_by_subject() to retrieve the email message object. Then extracts the subject from the message headers by searching for the header with name 'subject'. Extracts the pre-header text by searching for the 'x-preheader-text' header. Defines an inner recursive function find_html_part() that traverses the message payload structure to locate the HTML part of the email by checking mimeType for 'text/html' and recursively searching through nested parts. Once the HTML data is found, decodes it from base64 URL-safe encoding using base64.urlsafe_b64decode() and converts to UTF-8 string. Returns a dictionary with three keys: 'subject' containing the email subject line, 'pre_header' containing the pre-header text, and 'html_body' containing the decoded HTML content of the email body.
functions.usage_example:
details = EmailHelper.extract_email_details('user@test.com', 'Welcome Email')
subject = details['subject']
html = details['html_body']

Enters a while loop that continues until either the printer-selection page is reached or the timeout expires. In each iteration, attempts to start enrollment by calling start_enrollment(), logs the tenant email, checks if the URL contains 'printer-selection' and breaks if found, otherwise calls HPIDHelper.complete_sign_in() with the tenant email and default password to perform the sign-in. functions.what_it_does: Fills and submits the shipping address form during enrollment. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance and retrieves the address data from GlobalState.address_payload at the specified index. Extracts the full state name from the address. Optionally clicks the add_shipping_button if it's visible. Fills the company name if provided. Fills street1, street2, city (if country is not in the no-city-mandate list), state (if country requires states - uses dropdown selection), zip code (if country requires it), and phone number fields with data from the address object. If additional_check is True, validates that the receive updates message is visible, checks that the receive updates checkbox is unchecked by default (raises exception if checked), verifies close button, cancel button, and save button are all visible. Clicks the save button to submit the form. Handles the suggested address modal if it appears by clicking ship_to_address_button. If additional_check is True, validates that the entered address details are visible on the page after saving. Uses callback for screenshots if provided when errors occur.

functions.what_it_does: Authenticates and accesses the Fulfillment Service Rails Admin interface. Navigates to the Rails Admin login URL by concatenating common._fulfillmentservice_url with common._agena_cps_and_fulfillment_rails_admin_login_url with a 120 second timeout. Checks if an alert warning indicating the user is already signed in is visible. If already signed in, logs an info message and clicks the home link to go to the main page. If not signed in, fills the admin email field with common._rails_admin_user, fills the admin password field with common._rails_admin_password, clicks the submit button, and waits for the network to be idle with a 120 second timeout. If the URL is still the login URL after submission (indicating login failed), retries the login using credentials from common._client_secrets. Finally clicks the home link to navigate to the main Rails Admin page.

functions.what_it_does: Performs a complete order processing workflow in the Fulfillment Simulator. First calls access() to log into the Fulfillment Simulator Rails Admin. Calls access_order_by_trigger_id() to navigate to the specific order. Calls update_order() to update the order status and tracking information. Then triggers background job processing by calling RABaseHelper.complete_jobs() twice - first to complete the 'CheckSqsOrderStatusUpdateJob' which checks for order status updates from SQS, and second to complete the 'GeminiCallbackDispatcherJob' which dispatches callbacks to Gemini. This simulates the complete fulfillment flow including asynchronous job processing.

If already signed in, logs an info message and returns early without attempting login. If not signed in, types the admin email from common._rails_admin_user into the email field, types the admin password from common._rails_admin_password into the password field, and clicks the submit button to log in.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance

parameters.name: kit_type
parameters.description: Type of kit to ship - either 'welcome' or 'replenishment'. Defaults to 'welcome'.

parameters.name: colors
parameters.description: List of color codes to ship. Valid values are 'K', 'CMY', 'C', 'M', 'Y'. If None, no colors are selected.

functions.what_it_does: Ships a cartridge kit (welcome or replenishment) with specified colors through Gemini Rails Admin. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to access the 'Ship it' menu. Asserts that kit_type is either 'welcome' or 'replenishment', raising an AssertionError if invalid. Sets the radio button for the specified kit type to checked. If colors list is provided, iterates through each color, asserts it is one of the valid values ('K', 'CMY', 'C', 'M', 'Y'), and checks the corresponding supply checkbox by converting the color to lowercase and appending '_supply'. Clicks the 'Ship Ink Now' submit button. Extracts the trigger ID from the alert message using regex to search for 'trigger id (\w+)' pattern. Returns the extracted trigger_id string or empty string if not found.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance

parameters.name: event_shift
parameters.description: Number of days to shift events forward (as a string)

parameters.name: force_billing
parameters.description: Whether to create a billing cycle after rollback. Defaults to True.

functions.what_it_does: Shifts subscription events forward in time by a specified number of days, used for time-based testing. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to access the Edit menu. Fills the subscription rollback field '#subscription_rollback' with the event_shift value. Waits for the create billing cycle checkbox to be present. If force_billing is True, clicks the checkbox to enable billing cycle creation after rollback and waits to verify it is checked. Clicks the primary save button to submit the changes. Waits for a success alert to appear with a 10 second timeout and asserts it is visible to confirm the event shift was successful.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance

functions.what_it_does: Submits a charge for a billing cycle in Gemini Rails Admin. Calls RABaseHelper.access_menu_of_page() to access the 'Submit Charge' menu option. Waits for the warning button to be visible with a 120 second timeout and clicks it to confirm the charge submission. Waits for a success alert to appear with a 150 second timeout and asserts that the alert text contains 'Billing cycle has been charged' to confirm the charge was successfully submitted.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The field title to verify

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The expected value for the field

parameters.name: retry
parameters.description: Whether to retry verification with page reloads. Defaults to True.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific field in Rails Admin contains the expected value with retry logic. Defines an inner operation function that calls RABaseHelper.get_field_text_by_title() to get the field value, asserts it equals the expected value with a descriptive error message, logs success, and returns True. Defines an on_retry function that logs a retry message, reloads the page, and waits for load state. Sets max_attempts to 12 if retry is True, otherwise 1. Calls common.retry_operation() with the operation function, operation name, max attempts, 10 second delay, and the on_retry callback if retry is enabled. This provides robust verification with automatic retries and page reloads for eventually consistent data.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where country selector popup may appear

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on the HPID page. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page object. Uses a try-except block to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the button is not found or any exception occurs during the click, the exception is silently caught and passed, allowing the flow to continue without interruption. This is a defensive method used to handle optional UI elements that may or may not be present.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID sign-in page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HP ID authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HP ID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the HP ID sign-in process with provided credentials. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password entry screen. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This method handles the two-step HPID login flow where username and password are entered on separate screens.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HP ID authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HP ID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HP ID sign-in flow including handling optional country selection popup. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup that may appear. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the sign-in option button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000), then clicks it to navigate to the sign-in form. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to complete the actual authentication with credentials. This is a composite method that combines multiple steps into a complete sign-in workflow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID sign-up page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new HP ID account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new HP ID account

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new HP ID account by filling out the sign-up form with provided credentials and default user information. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with the hardcoded value 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email parameter using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password parameter using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID verification page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where the verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Confirms the HP ID account by retrieving and entering the verification code sent to the user's email. Creates an HPIDPage instance and waits for the submit code button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Sets up a retry mechanism with 3 attempts and 10-second wait intervals. In each retry loop, waits for the specified timeout using page.wait_for_timeout(wait_time * 1000), then calls common.fetch_verification_code(email) to retrieve the verification code from the email. If a verification code is successfully retrieved, fills the code input field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code), clicks the submit code button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(), and logs a success message with the email, stack, country code, and language code from GlobalState. If the code retrieval fails, logs a warning and retries. If all retries fail, logs an error message. Finally waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the HPID page

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant account to be created

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HP ID sign-up flow including country selection dismissal, account creation, and email verification. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance and waits for the create account button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000), then clicks it to navigate to the sign-up form. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill out and submit the registration form using the provided tenant email and the default password from the common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to retrieve the verification code from email and complete the account verification process. This is a high-level composite method that orchestrates the entire new user onboarding workflow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to navigate to the landing page

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Instant Ink landing page URL. Uses page.goto() to navigate to the URL stored in common._instantink_url with a 120-second timeout. After navigation completes, waits for the page load state to reach 'load' using page.wait_for_load_state('load') to ensure the page is fully loaded before proceeding. This is a basic navigation method that does not handle any popups or banners.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to access the landing page

functions.what_it_does: Accesses the landing page and handles common popups and banners that may appear. First calls LandingPageHelper.open(page) to navigate to the landing page URL. Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls privacy_banner_page.accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy consent banner if it appears. Creates a LandingPage instance and attempts to close any promotional banner by clicking landing_page.close_banner using a try-except block. If the close banner button is not found or cannot be clicked, the exception is caught and a message is printed. This method provides a clean landing page state ready for testing by dismissing common overlays.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance where the RaF banner should be visible

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Number of free months expected to be displayed in the RaF banner text. Defaults to 1.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) banner is visible on the landing page and contains the expected text about free months. Creates a LandingPage instance and locates the RaF banner element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_banner). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30-second timeout. Retrieves the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is present in the actual banner text using a Python assert statement with a descriptive error message. Logs a success message with a checkmark using framework_logger.info() if the verification passes. Returns True upon successful verification.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance where the RaF footnote should be visible

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) footnote is visible on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance and locates the RaF footnote element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_footnote). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the footnote element is visible with a 30-second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to evaluate JavaScript on

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check viewport visibility

functions.what_it_does: Verifies whether a page section is currently visible within the browser viewport. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript code in the browser context. The JavaScript code takes the section_selector as a parameter, uses document.querySelector() to find the element, returns false if the element is not found, otherwise gets the element's bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect(). Checks if the element's top edge is at or below the viewport top (rect.top >= 0) and if the element's bottom edge is at or above the viewport bottom (rect.bottom <= window.innerHeight or document.documentElement.clientHeight). Returns true if both conditions are met, meaning the section is fully visible in the viewport, otherwise returns false. This method is useful for validating that anchor link navigation scrolls the target section into view.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to capture screenshots or perform custom actions. Called with (step_name, page, screenshot_only=True) signature.

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete consumer landing page flow for desktop (OOBE - Out Of Box Experience) viewport. Navigates to the hardcoded stage1 landing page URL. Attempts to accept the privacy banner using a try-except block with a 10-second timeout. Waits for the sign-up header button to be visible with a 200-second timeout, then clicks it. Attempts to accept the privacy banner again after navigation. Waits for the HPID page to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Calls the optional callback function for the signup_function step if provided. Logs success for the sign-up function. Returns to the landing page URL and accepts the privacy banner again. Tests the How It Works section by clicking the tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the section is visible, checking if it's in viewport using verify_section_is_in_viewport(), logging the result, and calling the callback. Repeats similar testing for Instant Paper Plan section, Plans section, and FAQs section. Each section test includes clicking the navigation tab, waiting for scroll, verifying visibility, checking viewport position, logging results, and calling the callback. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs any errors encountered.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to capture screenshots or perform custom actions. Called with (step_name, page, screenshot_only=True) signature.

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete consumer landing page flow for mobile (MOBE - Mobile Experience) viewport. Similar to consumer_landing_page_oobe but adapted for mobile navigation patterns. Navigates to the hardcoded stage1 landing page URL. Attempts to accept the privacy banner. Waits for the mobile menu button to be visible with a 200-second timeout, then clicks it to open the mobile menu. Waits for the mobile sign-up button to be visible, then clicks it. Attempts to accept the privacy banner again. Waits for the HPID page to be visible. Calls the optional callback for the signup_function step. Logs success. Returns to the landing page and opens the mobile menu again. Tests the How It Works section by clicking the mobile tab, waiting for scroll, verifying visibility and viewport position, logging results, and calling the callback. Opens the mobile menu again before testing each subsequent section. Tests Instant Paper Plan section, Plans section, and FAQs section using the same pattern - opening mobile menu, clicking the mobile-specific tab, waiting for scroll, verifying visibility and viewport position, logging results, and calling the callback. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs any errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots or custom actions

functions.what_it_does: Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section on the landing page for desktop viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the never_worry_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout using landing_page.never_worry_section.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=50000). Scrolls the section into view if needed using landing_page.never_worry_section.scroll_into_view_if_needed(timeout=50000). Logs a success message using framework_logger.info(). Calls the optional callback function with the step name 'never_worry' if provided. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs any errors using framework_logger.error().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots or custom actions

functions.what_it_does: Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section on the landing page for mobile viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the mobe_never_work_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout using landing_page.mobe_never_work_section.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=50000). Scrolls the section into view if needed using landing_page.mobe_never_work_section.scroll_into_view_if_needed(timeout=50000). Logs a success message using framework_logger.info(). Calls the optional callback function with the step name 'never_worry' if provided. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs any errors using framework_logger.error().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport hyperlink testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots of opened pages

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for desktop viewport by clicking each link and verifying the destination URL. Creates a LandingPage instance. For each footer link (HP.com, Privacy Statement, HP Connected Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Your Privacy Choices, Instant Ink Terms of Service), the method waits for the link to be visible, scrolls it into view if needed, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the link is clicked, brings the new page to front, waits for it to load with a 60-second timeout, uses Playwright's expect() with a regex pattern to verify the URL matches the expected destination, logs success, calls the optional callback with the new page for screenshots, and closes the new tab. For some links like Privacy Statement and Cookie Settings, adds a 5-second sleep before verification to allow full page load. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs any errors using framework_logger.error().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport hyperlink testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots of opened pages

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for mobile viewport. Identical to hyperlinks_validation() but sets the viewport size to mobile dimensions (400x740) for each new page opened using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}). Creates a LandingPage instance. For each footer link (HP.com, Privacy Statement, HP Connected Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Your Privacy Choices, Instant Ink Terms of Service), waits for visibility, scrolls into view, captures the new tab with page.context.expect_page(), sets mobile viewport size, waits for load, verifies URL with regex pattern, logs success, calls callback, and closes the tab. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for desktop viewport cookie settings testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Validates the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for desktop viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the footer cookies link to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the cookies link is clicked. Waits for the new page to load with a 60-second timeout. Verifies the URL matches the HP cookie policy page using expect().to_have_url() with a regex pattern. Logs success. Scrolls the cookies preference button into view if needed using new_page.locator().scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Waits for the cookies preference button to be visible and clicks it. Logs success. Waits 5 seconds for the cookie preference modal to appear using time.sleep(5). Retrieves the cookie preference title text using new_page.locator().text_content(). Verifies the title equals 'Privacy Preference Center' and logs the result. Waits for the close button to be visible. Calls the optional callback with the new page for screenshots. Clicks the close button to dismiss the modal. Logs success. Closes the new tab. All operations are wrapped in a try-except block that logs errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for mobile viewport cookie settings testing

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Validates the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for mobile viewport. Identical to cookies_settings_oobe() but sets the viewport size to mobile dimensions (400x740) using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}) after opening the new tab. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the footer cookies link. Captures the new tab. Sets mobile viewport size. Waits for load. Verifies URL. Logs success. Scrolls cookies preference button into view. Waits for visibility and clicks it. Logs success. Waits 5 seconds for modal. Retrieves and verifies title text. Waits for close button. Calls callback. Clicks close button. Logs success. Closes tab. All wrapped in try-except with error logging. Note: The method has a stray line 'expect(raf_footnote).to_be_visible(timeout=30000)' at the end which appears to be a copy-paste error and is not related to cookie settings.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for FAQ link validation

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ links on the landing page by iterating through FAQ sections and clicking each link to verify it opens correctly. Creates a LandingPage instance and stores the original page URL. Scrolls the FAQs section into view and verifies it's visible. Defines a list of FAQ sections with tuples containing section name and section ID: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans. For each section, logs the section name, navigates back to the original URL if needed, locates the section tab using data-testid and aria-controls attributes, clicks the tab, waits 500ms. Checks if a 'Show more' button exists and clicks it if present. Locates all question buttons within the section using a selector for buttons with aria-controls attributes. Iterates through each question by index. For each question, re-locates the questions list, clicks the question button, waits 300ms, gets the aria-controls ID, locates the answer element by ID, verifies the answer is visible with a 3-second timeout. Locates all links within the answer. For each link, re-locates the answer and links, gets the link href attribute, skips mailto and tel links. Clicks the link, waits 2 seconds, gets all pages in the context, brings the last page to front, waits for networkidle with 35-second timeout. Tries to verify HP branding image is visible with 3-second timeout and logs success, or if that fails, verifies the URL contains '/terms' with 30-second timeout and logs success. Closes the new page. Navigates back to original URL, waits for domcontentloaded, re-opens the section tab, clicks show more if needed, re-opens the question. After processing all links in a question, closes the question. Uses extensive try-except blocks to handle failures gracefully and continue testing remaining items. Logs warnings for failed links and sections.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for country selection

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to select from the country selector. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country from the country selector on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance and clicks the flag icon to open the country selector using landing_page.flag_icon.click(). Loads the locale data mapping using load_locale_data() which returns a dictionary mapping country names to locale information. Retrieves the languages list and country code for the specified country from the locale map. Gets the first language from the languages list. Converts the country code to lowercase. Constructs the locale code string in the format 'language-countrycode' (e.g., 'en-us'). Locates the country selector element using the data-testid attribute with the locale code and clicks it to select the country.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for emergency banner validation

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to validate the emergency banner for. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Validates the emergency banner on the landing page has the correct background color and icon for the specified country. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the emergency banner's parent element using landing_page.emergency_banner.locator('..'). Calls common.get_font_details(banner_element) to retrieve CSS properties including background color. Extracts the detected background color from the returned data. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected_color (RGB string), and expected_icon_path (SVG path data or None). Retrieves the configuration for the target country from the dictionary. Logs the country and banner name being tested. Asserts the detected color matches the expected color using a Python assert statement with a descriptive error message. Logs success. If the expected_icon_path is not None (for countries like Finland, Portugal, Australia, Norway, Poland, Estonia), locates the SVG path element within the emergency banner, gets its 'd' attribute value, and asserts it matches the expected SVG path data. Logs success. If expected_icon_path is None (for Bulgaria with orange banner), locates the element with class 'orangeBannerIcon', asserts its count is greater than 0, and logs success. Finally logs overall validation success for the banner type.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to navigate to the OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the OSS emulator URL. Uses page.goto() to navigate to the URL stored in common._oss_url. Waits for the page load state to reach 'load' with a 60-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=60000) to ensure the emulator page is fully loaded before proceeding.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Signs in to the OSS emulator using HPID authentication. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dev tab using oss_emulator_page.dev_tab.click() to navigate to the developer options. Then clicks the OSS HPID login button using oss_emulator_page.oss_hpid_login.click() to initiate the HPID sign-in flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the 3-month trial button (Yeti 3M) in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) to allow the page to stabilize. Then clicks the yeti_3m button with a 60-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.yeti_3m.click(timeout=60000).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard (base64 encoded), and fingerprint (base64 encoded) attributes

functions.what_it_does: Fills the OSS emulator form with printer configuration details. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Fills the serial number field with printer.entity_id using oss_emulator_page.serial_number.fill(). Fills the SKU field with printer.model_number. Fills the UUID field with printer.device_uuid. Decodes the base64-encoded postcard value using base64.b64decode(printer.postcard).decode('utf-8') and fills the cloud_id field. Decodes the base64-encoded fingerprint value using base64.b64decode(printer.fingerprint).decode('utf-8') and fills the first fingerprint field. Selects 'completed' option for the language_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.language_config.select_option('completed'). Selects 'completed' option for the country_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.country_config.select_option('completed').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

parameters.name: app_type
parameters.description: App type to select in the emulator. If None, uses common._simulator_platform or defaults to 'Gotham'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects the app type in the OSS emulator. Determines the app type to use by first checking if the app_type parameter is provided, otherwise uses common._simulator_platform, or defaults to 'Gotham' if neither is available. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app tab using oss_emulator_page.app_tab.click() to navigate to the app configuration section. Selects the determined app type from the app_type dropdown using oss_emulator_page.app_type.select_option(app_type).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Starts the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the start flow button using oss_emulator_page.start_flow_button.click() to initiate the printer setup flow in the emulator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object with configuration details

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow by orchestrating multiple helper methods. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month trial option. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill in all printer configuration details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app type. Finally calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the setup flow. This is a high-level composite method that combines all setup steps into a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

functions.what_it_does: Configures the OSS emulator for Post Out Of Box Experience (POOBE) flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_post_oobe_agreements_features option using oss_emulator_page.app_post_oobe_agreements_features.click() to enable POOBE mode. Clicks the subscription_checkbox using oss_emulator_page.subscription_checkbox.click() to enable subscription features. Clicks the oobe_checkbox using oss_emulator_page.oobe_checkbox.click() to enable OOBE features.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the OSS emulator

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object with configuration details

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow for POOBE (Post Out Of Box Experience) scenarios. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.setup_poobe_flow(page) to configure POOBE-specific settings. Finally calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the flow. This is a composite method specifically for POOBE testing scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for accepting connected printing services

functions.what_it_does: Accepts the connected printing services agreement. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for the page to fully load with a 120-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=120000). Then clicks the accept all button using oss_emulator_page.accept_all_button.click() to accept all connected printing services terms.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for declining HP Plus

functions.what_it_does: Declines the HP Plus offer during printer setup. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the last 'do not activate HP Plus' option with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.do_not_activate_hp_plus.last.click(timeout=120000). Then clicks the decline HP Plus button using oss_emulator_page.decline_hp_plus.click() to confirm the decline action.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for continuing past dynamic security notice
