functions.what_it_does: Continues past the dynamic security notice screen. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dynamic security continue button with a 200-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.ds_continue_button.click(timeout=200000) to proceed past the security notice.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for continuing past value proposition

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the value proposition screen. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the next button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.next_button.click(timeout=120000) to proceed past the value proposition presentation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for selecting replace printer option

functions.what_it_does: Selects the replace printer option and continues. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the second replace printer radio button using oss_emulator_page.replace_printer_radio_button.nth(1).click() to select the replace printer option. Then clicks the continue button using oss_emulator_page.continue_button.click() to proceed with the printer replacement flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for activating HP Plus

functions.what_it_does: Activates HP Plus during printer setup. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the HP Plus continue button using oss_emulator_page.Hp_Plus_Continue_button.click() to proceed to the activation screen. Then clicks the Activate HP Plus button using oss_emulator_page.Activate_HP_Plus.click() to confirm and activate HP Plus.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for country selection

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country in the OSS emulator based on GlobalState configuration. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Locates the country selector element using the country code from GlobalState.country_code with the selector pattern f"[id='{GlobalState.country_code}']". Wraps the selection logic in a try-except block. Scrolls the country selector into view if needed using select_country.scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Checks if the country selector is visible with a 100-second timeout. If visible, logs the country code using framework_logger.info(), clicks the country selector, and clicks the continue selection button using oss_emulator_page.continue_selection.click(). Waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10) after selection. If any exception occurs, the except block passes silently allowing the flow to continue.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for verification

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Number of months expected in the trial text

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the reminder checkbox is visible, checked, and contains the correct trial text. Locates the reminder checkbox using the data-testid 'instant-ink-reminder'. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the checkbox is visible with a 500-second timeout. Verifies the checkbox is checked with a 2-second timeout using expect().to_be_checked(). Locates the reminder text element using the class selector starting with 'styles__CheckBoxText'. Uses expect().to_have_text() with a regex pattern to verify the text matches 'Remind me 2 weeks before my {expected_months}-month trial of HP Instant Ink expires.' where the expected_months value is interpolated into the pattern. Locates the trial info disclaimer using data-testid 'general-monthly-fee-disclaimer'. Verifies it is visible. Verifies it has the exact text 'After the trial, a monthly fee will be automatically charged unless canceled.' using expect().to_have_text().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for accessing learn more

functions.what_it_does: Accesses the learn more page by clicking the learn more button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the learn more button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.learn_more_button.click(timeout=120000) to navigate to additional information about the service.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k', 'c,m,y', 'cmy')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that cartridge installed messages are visible on the overview page for specified cartridge types. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get a list of cartridge types. Defines a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes ('k', 'c', 'm', 'y', 'cmy') to their corresponding selector strings retrieved from overview_page.get_selector(). Iterates through each cartridge type in the list. For each cartridge type, strips whitespace, checks if it exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary, and raises a RuntimeError if invalid. Waits for the cartridge element to be visible with a 20-second timeout using page.wait_for_selector(). Asserts that the cartridge element is visible using assert cartridge_element.is_visible() with a descriptive error message. This method validates that the correct cartridge status indicators are displayed on the overview page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: First cartridge type or combination to try verifying

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Second cartridge type or combination to try if first fails

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to verify cartridge installed messages for multiple cartridge type combinations with fallback logic. Uses a try-except block to first attempt verifying the cartridge parameter by calling OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge). If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the first cartridge type is invalid or not found), catches the exception and attempts to verify the cartridge2 parameter by calling OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2). This method is useful when a printer may have different cartridge configurations and you want to verify either configuration is valid.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Name of the printer status group section to check (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive', 'Needs Attention')

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Expected number of printers - either 'only one' or 'more than one'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the number of printers in a specific status group section on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Retrieves all printer status section elements using overview_page.printers_grouped_by_status.all(). Initializes printers_grouped to None. Iterates through each section element checking if the group parameter text is present in the element's inner text. If found, assigns that element to printers_grouped and breaks the loop. Asserts that printers_grouped is not None and is visible, with an error message if the section is not found. Asserts that the group text is present in the section's inner text. Locates all printer selector option elements within the section using overview_page.printer_selector_option and gets them as a list. If number parameter is 'only one', asserts the list length equals 1. If number parameter is 'more than one', asserts the list length is greater than 1. If number parameter is neither, raises an Exception with 'Invalid number of printers'. This method validates printer grouping and counting on the overview page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

parameters.name: banner_type
parameters.description: Type of plan suggestion banner to verify - either 'downgrading' or 'upgrading'. Defaults to 'downgrading'.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a plan suggestion banner is visible on the overview page and contains appropriate text for the specified banner type. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets the plan suggestion banner selector using overview_page.get_selector('plan_suggestion_banner'). Locates the banner element using page.locator(). Waits for the banner to be visible with a 30-second timeout using banner_locator.wait_for(state='visible', timeout=30000). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible. Retrieves the banner text content and converts it to lowercase. If banner_type is 'downgrading', defines a list of downgrade keywords ('downgrading', 'lower plan', 'reduce', 'save money', 'smaller plan') and asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text using any() and a list comprehension. If banner_type is 'upgrading', defines a list of upgrade keywords ('upgrading', 'higher plan', 'more pages', 'increase', 'larger plan') and asserts at least one is present. If banner_type is neither, raises a ValueError. Prints a success message with a checkmark. Returns the banner_locator for further use.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: Expected number of pages in the upgraded plan as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the overview page displays the correct upgraded plan page count. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the plan_information element is visible with a 120-second timeout. Retrieves the inner text of the plan_information element using overview_page.plan_information.inner_text(). Removes periods and commas from the text using replace() to normalize the format. Uses re.findall() with the pattern r'\d+' to extract all numeric values from the text into a list. Gets the second number from the list (index 1) which represents the plan pages. Asserts that the extracted plan pages value as a string equals the provided plan parameter as a string. This method validates that plan upgrade changes are correctly reflected on the overview page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the unsubscribed status card is displayed with all expected action buttons. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify three elements are visible: the 'Enroll printer again' button with a 150-second timeout using expect(overview_page.unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button).to_be_visible(timeout=150000), the 'HP Shop Ink' button with a 30-second timeout, and the 'Download past invoices' link with a 30-second timeout. This method validates that users who have unsubscribed see the appropriate options to re-enroll, purchase ink, or access their invoice history.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the HP Shop Ink button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies it navigates to the correct shop page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the button is clicked. Clicks the HP Shop Ink button using overview_page.unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button.click(). Gets the new tab page object from the context. Uses expect() with a regex pattern to verify the new tab's URL contains '/shop/pdp' with a 30-second timeout. Closes the new tab using new_tab.close(). This method validates that the HP Shop Ink link correctly navigates to the HP ink product page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the 'Enroll printer again' button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies the enrollment flow starts correctly. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Clicks the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button using overview_page.unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button.click(). Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new enrollment page that opens. Gets the new page object and brings it to front using enrollment_page.bring_to_front(). Uses a try-except block to attempt accepting automatic printer updates by calling EnrollmentHelper.accept_automatic_printer_updates(enrollment_page) and logs success if the page appears. If the page is not found, logs that it was already passed. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance with the enrollment page. Uses expect() to verify the v3_content_area element is visible with a 120-second timeout, confirming the enrollment flow reached the confirmation page. Closes the enrollment page using enrollment_page.close(). This method validates the re-enrollment flow for previously unsubscribed users.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the overview page

parameters.name: invoices_directory
parameters.description: Directory path where the invoice PDF should be saved. Defaults to '/tmp/downloads'.

functions.what_it_does: Downloads past invoices from the unsubscribed status card and verifies the PDF file is created. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link is visible with a 30-second timeout. Creates the invoices directory if it doesn't exist using os.makedirs(invoices_directory, exist_ok=True). Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new PDF page that opens when the download link is clicked. Clicks the download past invoices link using overview_page.unsubscribed_download_past_invoices.click(). Gets the PDF page object and brings it to front using pdf_page.bring_to_front(). Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the PDF to load. Constructs the PDF file path using os.path.join(invoices_directory, 'invoice.pdf'). Saves the PDF using pdf_page.pdf(path=pdf_file). Closes the PDF page using pdf_page.close(). Waits 2 seconds. Asserts that the PDF file exists at the expected path using os.path.exists() with a descriptive error message. Takes a screenshot of the page and saves it to the invoices directory. Logs success with the PDF file path using framework_logger.info().

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle period changes the display from 'Current' to a past period. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing cycle dropdown using print_history_page.billing_cycle_option.click(). Clicks the first previous period option using print_history_page.select_1_option.click(). Uses Playwright's expect() with not_to_contain_text() to verify that the billing_cycle_period_title element no longer contains the text 'Current', confirming that a previous billing period is now displayed.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: period_index
parameters.description: Index of the billing period to select (1, 2, or 3)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period from the dropdown by index. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing cycle dropdown to open it using print_history_page.billing_cycle_option.click(). Logs that the dropdown was opened using framework_logger.info(). Uses conditional logic to select the appropriate period option based on period_index: if period_index is 1, clicks select_1_option; if 2, clicks select_2_option; if 3, clicks select_3_option; otherwise raises a ValueError with a message indicating valid values are 1, 2, or 3. Logs which period option was selected. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with a 10-second timeout, confirming the table has updated after the period selection. Logs that the billing table was updated.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: total_pages_printed
parameters.description: Expected total number of pages printed to verify

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the billing table displays the correct total pages printed value. Logs that billing table data validation is starting using framework_logger.info(). Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Retrieves the inner text of the total_pages_printed element using print_history_page.total_pages_printed.inner_text(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the text into a list. If the list is empty (no numbers found), raises a ValueError with a message showing the expected value and the actual text. Asserts that the first number in the list (index 0) equals the total_pages_printed parameter, with a descriptive error message showing expected vs actual values if they don't match.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Either a Playwright locator object or a string CSS selector

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Retrieves text content from a page element, handling both locator objects and string selectors. Uses hasattr() to check if the selector parameter has an 'inner_text' attribute. If it does (meaning it's already a Playwright locator object), calls selector.inner_text() directly and returns the result. If it doesn't (meaning it's a string selector), uses page.locator(selector) to create a locator, calls .first to get the first matching element, calls .inner_text() to get the text, and returns it. This utility method provides flexibility in how selectors are passed to other methods.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize - can be string, number, or None

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values to a consistent format for comparison. If the value is falsy (None, empty string, etc.), returns '0.00'. Converts the value to a string, strips whitespace, replaces commas with periods for decimal normalization, removes dollar signs, euro signs, and pound signs using replace(). Removes non-breaking spaces (\xa0) and regular spaces. If the normalized value is a dash, em dash, 'N/A', or 'n/a', returns '0.00'. Uses a try-except block to attempt converting the normalized string to a float using float(). If successful, formats it as a string with 2 decimal places using f'{float_val:.2f}' and returns it. If conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This method handles various currency formats and edge cases to enable reliable currency value comparisons.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Locator object or string selector for the currency field

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value - can be int (cents), float, or string

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field contains the expected value with flexible matching options. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector(page, selector) to retrieve the actual text from the element. Checks if the expected value is an integer using isinstance(). If it is, divides it by 100 to convert from cents to dollars (e.g., 1299 becomes 12.99). Calls PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value(expected) to normalize the expected value to a consistent format. If partial_match is True, replaces commas with periods in the actual text for normalization, then asserts that the normalized expected value is contained in the actual text using the 'in' operator, with a descriptive error message if not found. If partial_match is False, calls PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value(actual_text) to normalize the actual value, then asserts that the normalized actual value exactly equals the normalized expected value, with a descriptive error message showing both normalized and raw values if they don't match. This method handles various currency formats and provides flexible validation for exact or partial matches.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected values for billing section fields. Keys can include: total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies multiple fields in the billing section against expected values provided in a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each possible field in the expected_values dictionary, checks if the key exists and performs validation. For 'total_pages_printed', extracts numbers from the total_pages_printed element text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(), gets the first number, and asserts it equals the expected value. For 'pages', extracts numbers from the plan_pages_text element using get_text_from_selector() and common.extract_numbers_from_text(), gets the first number, and asserts equality. For 'rollover', checks if the expected value is not a dash, then extracts numbers from rollover_pages_text and asserts equality. For 'additional', checks if the expected value is not a dash, then extracts numbers from additional_pages_text and asserts equality. For 'plan_price', calls validate_currency_field() with plan_price_text selector and partial_match=True. For 'overage_price', calls validate_currency_field() with overage_description_text selector and partial_match=True. For 'previous', calls validate_currency_field() with previous_billing_text selector for exact match. For 'charges', calls validate_currency_field() with current_billing_text selector for exact match. For 'tax', checks if expected value is not a dash, then uses a try-except block to call validate_currency_field() with the tax-amount data-testid selector, logging a warning if the element is not found. For 'total', calls validate_currency_field() with current_total_text selector for exact match. This comprehensive method validates all billing section data points in a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing 'billing_cycle' key (1-based index) and expected values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Verifies billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and validating each cycle. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Iterates through each row dictionary in the expected_data list. For each row, extracts the 'billing_cycle' value, converts it to an integer, and subtracts 1 to get a 0-based index. Logs which billing cycle is being validated using framework_logger.info(). Clicks the billing cycle dropdown to open it using print_history_page.billing_cycle_option.click(). Logs that the dropdown was opened. Locates all billing cycle options using page.locator(print_history_page.elements.billing_cycle_options). Clicks the option at the calculated index using billing_options.nth(billing_cycle_index).click(). Logs which option was selected. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout(1000) for the page to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section(page, row) to verify all the expected values for that billing cycle. This method enables data-driven testing of multiple billing cycles with a single method call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ questions and their embedded links on the print and payment history page. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Clicks the first FAQ question using print_history_page.faq_question_1.click(). Uses expect() to verify the first FAQ answer is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab that opens when the Terms of Service link is clicked. Clicks the Terms of Service link in the first FAQ answer. Verifies the popup URL contains '/terms' using expect().to_have_url() with a regex pattern and 30-second timeout. Closes the popup. Clicks the second FAQ question. Verifies the second FAQ answer is visible. Clicks the Overview link in the second FAQ answer. Verifies the overview page status card title is visible with a 30-second timeout, confirming navigation to the overview page. Navigates back to print history using side_menu.print_history_menu_link.click(). Clicks the second FAQ question again. Verifies the answer is visible. Clicks the first Update Plan link in the second FAQ answer. Verifies the update plan page title is visible, confirming navigation to the update plan page. Navigates back to print history. Clicks the third FAQ question. Verifies the third FAQ answer is visible. Clicks the fourth FAQ question. Verifies the fourth FAQ answer is visible. Clicks the last Update Plan link in the fourth FAQ answer. Verifies the update plan page title is visible. Navigates back to print history. This comprehensive method tests all FAQ interactions including link navigation and page transitions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

functions.what_it_does: Validates the print history page state when no pens (printers) are enrolled. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that the print_history_section element is visible with a 30-second timeout. Verifies that the page_title element is visible. Uses expect() with not_to_be_visible() to verify that several elements are NOT visible: print_history_card_title, how_is_calculated_link, total_printed_pages, plan_pages_bar, and plan_details_card. This validates that the page shows a minimal state without detailed print history information when no printers are enrolled.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the download all invoices button with retry logic and verifies the invoices page opens. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. In each attempt, uses a try-except block to click the print_history_table_button using print_history_page.print_history_table_button.click(). Uses expect() to verify the download_all_invoices_button is visible with a 5-second timeout. If successful, breaks out of the loop. If an exception occurs and it's not the last attempt (59), logs a retry message with the attempt number using framework_logger.info(), waits 60 seconds using time.sleep(60), reloads the page using page.reload(), and waits for the print_history_card_title to be visible with a 90-second timeout. If all 60 attempts fail, raises an Exception indicating the button is not visible after maximum retries. After the button is visible, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab that opens when the download all invoices button is clicked. Clicks the button. Gets the new tab page object. Asserts that the word 'invoices' is present in the new tab's URL, with a descriptive error message showing the actual URL if the assertion fails. This method handles the case where the download button may take time to appear after page load.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text is present in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using print_history_page.print_history_table.is_hidden(). If it is hidden, clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table is visible with a 30-second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the table content to fully load. Retrieves the inner text of the entire print history table using print_history_page.print_history_table.inner_text(). Asserts that the notifications_text parameter is present in the table content using the 'in' operator, with a descriptive error message showing the expected text and actual table content if the assertion fails.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value as a string

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan pages value as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information displays the correct plan value and plan pages. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Retrieves the text content of the plan_price_text element using print_history_page.plan_price_text.text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the plan info text into a list. Asserts that the first number in the list (index 0) equals the plan_value parameter, with a descriptive error message showing expected vs actual if they don't match. Asserts that the second number in the list (index 1) equals the plan_pages parameter, with a descriptive error message showing expected vs actual if they don't match.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for the print history page

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected current total value as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total amount displayed on the print history page matches the expected value. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Retrieves the text content of the first current_total_text element using print_history_page.current_total_text.first.text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract all numeric values from the current total text into a list. Asserts that the first number in the list (index 0) equals the total_value parameter, with a descriptive error message showing expected vs actual if they don't match.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: page_title
parameters.description: The title text that should appear in the h1 heading to confirm page load

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds before throwing timeout error. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with a dynamic selector constructed using an f-string that combines the page_title parameter into the selector pattern 'h1:has-text("Details for {page_title}")'. The method will wait up to the specified timeout duration (default 60 seconds) for the element to appear. If the element does not appear within the timeout period, Playwright will raise a TimeoutError. This method is useful for ensuring that detail pages have fully loaded before proceeding with test actions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: base_url
parameters.description: The base URL of the application where Resque admin interface is hosted

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Resque schedule page by constructing the full URL from the provided base_url and appending '/resque/schedule' to it. Uses Playwright's page.goto() method to perform the navigation. Resque is a Redis-backed job queue system, and this method provides access to its web-based admin interface where scheduled jobs can be viewed and managed. This is typically used as a setup step before running or monitoring background jobs in the test automation framework.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: base_url
parameters.description: The base URL of the application where Resque admin interface is hosted

parameters.name: job_name
parameters.description: The exact name of the scheduled job to run

functions.what_it_does: Executes a scheduled Resque job by locating it in the schedule table and clicking its 'Queue now' button. First constructs a row selector using f-string to find the table row containing the exact job name using 'tr:has(td:text-is("{job_name}"))'. Then constructs a button selector by combining the row selector with the input element selector 'input[value="Queue now"]'. Waits for the button to be visible, then clicks it to queue the job immediately. After clicking, waits for the Queues page heading 'h1.wi:has-text("Queues")' to appear confirming the action. Then navigates to the Resque working page at '/resque/working' to monitor job execution. Finally calls wait_for_job_to_disappear() to wait until the job completes and is no longer listed in the working jobs table. This method orchestrates the complete flow of triggering a scheduled job and waiting for its completion.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: base_url
parameters.description: The base URL of the application where Resque admin interface is hosted

parameters.name: jobs
parameters.description: List of job names to execute in sequence. Defaults to empty list.

functions.what_it_does: Executes multiple Resque jobs in sequence by iterating through the provided jobs list. For each job in the list, it first calls access_resque() to navigate to the Resque schedule page, then calls run_job() to execute that specific job and wait for its completion. This method is useful for test scenarios that require multiple background jobs to be completed as preconditions, such as processing subscriptions, sending notifications, or updating data. The jobs are executed sequentially, with each job fully completing before the next one starts.

functions.method_signature: def wait_for_job_to_disappear(page, job_name: str, timeout: int = 60)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: job_name
parameters.description: The name of the job to monitor for completion

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in seconds before raising timeout error. Defaults to 60 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Monitors the Resque working jobs table and waits for a specific job to complete and disappear from the list of active workers. Uses a polling loop that runs until the timeout is reached. In each iteration, it waits for the 'table.workers' element to appear with a 5-second timeout. Then locates all table rows except the header using 'table.workers tbody tr:not(:first-child)'. Iterates through each row to check if the queue cell (td.queues) contains the job_name in its text content. If the job is found, sets job_found flag to True and breaks the inner loop. If job_found is False (meaning the job is no longer in the table), the method returns successfully indicating the job has completed. If the job is still present, waits 2 seconds, reloads the page, and waits for network idle state before checking again. If any exception occurs during the check (such as table not found), the method returns assuming the job has completed. If the timeout is reached and the job is still present, raises a RuntimeError with a descriptive message. This method is critical for ensuring background jobs complete before proceeding with test assertions.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: page_name
parameters.description: The exact text of the navigation link to click

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking on a navigation link with the specified page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the provided page_name. The XPath expression '//a[@class="nav-link" and normalize-space(text())="{page_name}"]' ensures that only links with the exact text (after whitespace normalization) are matched. Once the link is located, clicks it to navigate to the target page. This method is useful for navigating through the application's main navigation menu.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: menu
parameters.description: The text of the menu item to click

functions.what_it_does: Clicks on a menu item by locating an element with a class attribute starting with 'nav-item' that contains the specified menu text. Uses the CSS selector '[class^="nav-item"]:has-text("{menu}")' to find the menu element. Waits for the element to be visible with a very long timeout of 120000ms (2 minutes) before clicking it. This extended timeout suggests that menu items may take significant time to load or become interactive in the application. The method is designed for navigating through secondary or dropdown menu items within a page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text to search for in card headers

functions.what_it_does: Locates and returns a card body element by searching for a card with a specific title in its header. Constructs a combined CSS selector that matches a div.card element containing either an h5.card-header with exact text matching the title, or an h5.card-header with an anchor child whose text exactly matches the title. The selector pattern is 'div.card:has(h5.card-header:text-is("{title}"), h5.card-header a:text-is("{title}"))'. First attempts to locate the card using page.locator(). If the card count is 0, waits up to 10 seconds for the card to appear. If still not found after waiting, raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating the field with that title was not found. If the card is found, returns a locator for the card body element using card.locator('div.card-body'). This method is fundamental for extracting data from card-based UI layouts where information is organized in titled sections.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing links

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves all link elements from a card section identified by its title. First calls get_item_by_title() to locate the card body element for the specified title. Then uses items.locator('a') to find all anchor elements within that card body. If no links are found (count is 0), raises a ValueError indicating that no links were found in the section with that title. If links are found, returns the links locator object which can be used to interact with or extract information from the links. This method is useful for extracting navigation links, related resource links, or action links from titled card sections.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing links

functions.what_it_does: Extracts and returns a list of all href URL values from links in a card section identified by its title. First calls get_links_by_title() to retrieve all link elements from the specified card section. Then uses a list comprehension to iterate through each link by index, calling links.nth(i).get_attribute('href') to extract the href attribute value from each link. Returns a Python list containing all the extracted URL strings. This method is useful for collecting URLs for validation, comparison, or further navigation in test scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the field

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls get_item_by_title() to locate the card body element for the specified title. Then calls field.inner_text() to extract all visible text content from within that card body, including text from nested elements. Finally strips leading and trailing whitespace using .strip() and returns the cleaned text string. This method is useful for extracting field values, descriptions, or any text content from titled card sections for validation or data extraction purposes.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: link_text
parameters.description: The text content of the link to click (can be partial match)

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the link

functions.what_it_does: Clicks on a specific link within a card section by searching for a link whose text contains the specified link_text. First calls get_links_by_title() to retrieve all links from the card section with the specified title. Initializes a clicked flag to False. Then iterates through each link by index using range(links.count()). For each link, gets the link element using links.nth(i), extracts its text content using link.inner_text(), and strips whitespace. Checks if the provided link_text is contained within the link's text content using the 'in' operator. If a match is found, clicks the link using link.click(), sets clicked flag to True, and breaks out of the loop. After the loop, asserts that clicked is True, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if no matching link was found. Returns True if the link was successfully clicked. This method supports partial text matching, making it flexible for clicking links even when the exact full text is not known.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the boolean field

functions.what_it_does: Extracts a boolean value from a field by examining the Font Awesome icon class used to represent the boolean state. First calls get_item_by_title() to locate the card body for the specified title. Then locates the second span element within that field using field.locator('span').nth(1) and retrieves its class attribute using get_attribute('class'). Checks if the class string contains 'fa-check' (indicating a checkmark icon), and if so returns True. Checks if the class contains 'fa-times' (indicating an X or cross icon), and if so returns False. If neither icon class is found, returns None indicating an indeterminate or unknown state. This method is designed for UI patterns where boolean values are displayed using Font Awesome icons rather than text.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing links

parameters.name: text_filter
parameters.description: The text string to filter links by (partial match)

functions.what_it_does: Filters and returns a list of link elements whose text content contains the specified text_filter string. First calls get_links_by_title() to retrieve all links from the card section with the specified title. Initializes an empty filtered_links list. Then iterates through each link by index using range(all_links.count()). For each link, gets the link element using all_links.nth(i) and extracts its text content using link.inner_text(). Checks if the text_filter string is contained within the link text using the 'in' operator. If the filter matches, appends the link element to the filtered_links list. After processing all links, returns the filtered_links list containing only the link elements that matched the filter criteria. This method is useful for finding specific links within a section when there are multiple links and you need to filter by text content.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: current_date
parameters.description: Expected current date for cancel initiate date validation. Can be string in 'MMM DD, YYYY' format or datetime object.

parameters.name: final_bill_date
parameters.description: Expected final billing date for final charge date validation. Can be string in 'MMM DD, YYYY' format or datetime object.

functions.what_it_does: Validates template data displayed on a page by extracting JSON data from a pre element and comparing date values against expected dates. First locates a pre element within the page content using '[class="page-content"] pre' selector and extracts its text content. Parses the text as JSON using json.loads() to get a data dictionary. Creates a timezone offset of 3 hours using timedelta(hours=3). Extracts the 'cancel_initiate_date' epoch timestamp from the data, converts it to a datetime object using datetime.fromtimestamp(), and adds the timezone offset. Extracts the 'final_charge_date_start' epoch timestamp, converts it to datetime, and adds the timezone offset. Checks if current_date parameter is a string, and if so converts it to datetime using strptime with format '%b %d, %Y'. Does the same conversion for final_bill_date if it's a string. Asserts that the epoch_cancel_initiate_date formatted as 'MMM DD, YYYY' matches the current_date formatted the same way, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if they don't match. Asserts that the epoch_date_time_start formatted as 'MMM DD, YYYY' matches the final_bill_date formatted the same way, raising an AssertionError if they don't match. This method is critical for validating that cancellation and billing date calculations in template data are correct.

functions.method_signature: def access_link_by_title_with_retry(page, link_text, title, max_attempts=12, delay=10)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: link_text
parameters.description: The text content of the link to click (can be partial match)

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The exact title text of the card section containing the link

parameters.name: max_attempts
parameters.description: Maximum number of retry attempts before giving up. Defaults to 12.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Delay in seconds between retry attempts. Defaults to 10 seconds.

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click a link within a card section with automatic retry logic for handling timing issues or dynamic content loading. Uses the common.retry_operation() utility function to wrap the access_link_by_title() method call with retry behavior. Passes a lambda function that calls RABaseHelper.access_link_by_title(page, link_text, title) as the operation to retry. Provides the link_text as an identifier for logging. Configures max_attempts (default 12) and delay (default 10 seconds) for the retry behavior. Specifies page.reload as the on_retry callback, meaning the page will be reloaded before each retry attempt. This method is useful for scenarios where links may not be immediately available due to asynchronous data loading, background job completion, or other timing-dependent conditions. With default settings, it will retry for up to 2 minutes (12 attempts × 10 seconds) before failing.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k,c,m,y' or 'cmy'). Valid values are k, c, m, y, cmy.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that specific cartridge types are displayed as installed on the shipment tracking page. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter string by comma to get a list of individual cartridge types. Creates a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes ('k', 'c', 'm', 'y', 'cmy') to their corresponding selector strings retrieved from the page object using get_selector() method. Iterates through each cartridge type in the cartridges list. For each cartridge, strips whitespace and checks if it exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary. If an invalid cartridge type is provided, raises a RuntimeError with a descriptive message. For valid cartridge types, waits for the cartridge element to appear using page.wait_for_selector() with a 20-second timeout. Then asserts that the cartridge element is visible using is_visible(), raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if the cartridge is not visible. This method ensures that the expected cartridge installation messages are displayed correctly on the UI.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Primary comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Fallback comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify if primary fails

functions.what_it_does: Verifies cartridge installation messages with fallback logic for handling scenarios where different cartridge type configurations may be valid. First attempts to verify the primary cartridge configuration by calling verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge) within a try block. If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the primary cartridge types were not found or invalid), catches the exception and attempts to verify the fallback cartridge2 configuration by calling verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2). This method is useful for test scenarios where the application may display different cartridge configurations depending on printer model or subscription type, and either configuration is acceptable. For example, some printers may use individual K, C, M, Y cartridges while others use a combined CMY cartridge.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that all cartridge types for the current printer configuration are displayed as installed. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Creates a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping uppercase cartridge type codes ('K', 'C', 'M', 'Y', 'CMY') to their corresponding selector strings retrieved from the page object. Calls common.printer_colors() to get the list of cartridge colors applicable to the current printer being tested. Iterates through each cartridge type returned by printer_colors(). For each cartridge, strips whitespace and checks if it exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary. If an invalid cartridge type is encountered, raises a RuntimeError. For valid cartridge types, waits for the cartridge element to appear with a 20-second timeout, then asserts that it is visible. This method differs from verify_cartridge_installed_message in that it automatically determines which cartridges to verify based on the printer configuration rather than requiring explicit cartridge types to be passed as parameters.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the view entire history link or button on the shipment tracking page to expand or navigate to the full shipment history. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Waits for the shipment_history_table element to be visible with a 30-second timeout using wait_for() with state='visible'. Then clicks the shipment_history_table element to trigger the view history action. This method is used to access the complete shipment history before performing validations on the history data.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the contents of the shipment tracking history table by validating cartridge counts and welcome kit information. First creates a ShipmentTrackingPage instance. Waits for the shipment_history_table to be visible with a 30-second timeout, then clicks it to expand or access the history. Calls common.printer_colors() to get the list of cartridge colors for the current printer configuration. Waits for the shipment_history_description element to be visible with a 10-second timeout. Extracts the text content of the first description row using shipment_history_description.first.text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to parse numeric values from the text. Asserts that the number of extracted cartridge numbers matches the number of colors returned by printer_colors(). Asserts that the first cartridge number is '1'. Asserts that the second cartridge number is '1'. If there are 4 colors (indicating separate C, M, Y cartridges), also asserts that the third and fourth cartridge numbers are '1'. Then extracts the text content of the last description row using shipment_history_description.last.text_content() to get welcome kit information. Extracts numbers from the welcome kit text. Asserts that exactly 1 welcome kit number was found. Asserts that the welcome kit number is '1'. This comprehensive validation ensures that the shipment history correctly shows 1 cartridge of each type and 1 welcome kit.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that free trial information is not displayed on the update plan page. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Then uses Playwright's expect() assertion to verify that the free_trial_info_card element is not visible by calling expect(update_plan_page.free_trial_info_card).not_to_be_visible(). Also verifies that the free_trial_plan_details element is not visible using expect(update_plan_page.free_trial_plan_details).not_to_be_visible(). This method is used in test scenarios where the user should not see free trial offers, such as when they are already past the trial period or on a paid subscription.

functions.method_signature: def verify_available_plans(page, expectedPlans = [10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500], plan_type: str = None)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expectedPlans
parameters.description: List of expected plan page counts in the order they should appear. Defaults to [10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500].

parameters.name: plan_type
parameters.description: Type of plan to verify. If 'ink_and_paper', verifies that plans include ink and paper. If None, verifies that plans do not include ink and paper.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the correct set of subscription plans is displayed on the update plan page with expected page counts and plan types. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that the plan_card_container is visible with a 120-second timeout. Locates all plan card elements using the selector '[data-testid^="plans-selector-plan-card-container"]' and gets all matching elements with .all(). Counts the number of plan elements and asserts that it matches the length of expectedPlans list. Iterates through each plan card by index. For each card, extracts the text content and uses regex re.findall(r'\d+\.\d+|\d+', card_text) to extract all numeric values (including decimals). Takes the first number as the actual plan page count and converts it to integer. Compares it against the expected plan value from expectedPlans[i] and asserts they match. If plan_type is 'ink_and_paper', asserts that the text 'Includes ink and paper' is present in the card text. If plan_type is None, asserts that 'Includes ink and paper' is NOT present in the card text. This comprehensive validation ensures that the correct plans are displayed in the correct order with the correct page counts and plan type indicators.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expectedPlan
parameters.description: The page count of the plan that should be marked as current (e.g., 100 for 100-page plan)

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific plan is marked as the current active plan on the update plan page. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Calls update_plan_page.get_plan_by_pages(expectedPlan) to locate the plan card element corresponding to the specified page count. Within that plan card, locates the plan tag container element using the selector '[data-testid$="plan-tag-container"]'. Uses expect() to verify that this tag container element is visible, which indicates that the plan is marked as the current plan. The plan tag typically displays text like 'Current Plan' or a similar indicator. This method is used to verify that the correct plan is shown as active after plan selection or when viewing the current subscription state.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: The page count of the plan to select (e.g., 100 for 100-page plan)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a subscription plan by clicking the select button on the plan card with the specified page count. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Calls update_plan_page.get_plan_by_pages(plan_pages) to locate the plan card element for the specified page count. Within that plan card, locates the button element using plan_card.locator('button'). Clicks the button to select the plan. This action typically initiates a plan change or upgrade/downgrade process. The method is straightforward and assumes the plan card has a single button that performs the selection action.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_button
parameters.description: The index or identifier of the plan button to select

functions.what_it_does: Selects a plan by clicking a specific plan button identified by an index or identifier, with explicit wait handling for the button lifecycle. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Calls update_plan_page.get_select_plan_button(plan_button) to locate the specific plan button element. Waits for the button to be visible with a 90-second timeout using wait_for(state='visible'). Clicks the button to initiate the plan selection. Then waits for the button to be detached from the DOM with a 90-second timeout using wait_for(state='detached'), which indicates that the plan selection action has been processed and the UI has updated. This method provides more robust synchronization than select_plan by explicitly waiting for the button to disappear after clicking, ensuring the action has completed before proceeding.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: The 1-based position of the plan card to select (e.g., 1 for first plan, 2 for second plan)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a plan by clicking the button on the plan card at a specific position in the list of displayed plans. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Calls update_plan_page.get_plan_by_position(position-1) to locate the plan card at the specified position, subtracting 1 to convert from 1-based position to 0-based index. Within that plan card, locates the button element using plan_locator.locator('button'). Clicks the button to select the plan. This method is useful when the test needs to select a plan based on its display order rather than its page count, such as selecting the first plan, second plan, etc., regardless of what page counts those plans represent.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute when the cancellation confirmation page is displayed. Receives parameters (step_name, page, screenshot_only).

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete subscription cancellation flow including clicking the cancel link, confirming cancellation, and handling the cancellation feedback page. First creates UpdatePlanPage and CancellationPage instances. Waits for the cancel_instant_ink link to be visible with a 90-second timeout, then clicks it to initiate cancellation. Waits for the confirm_cancellation_button to be visible with a 120-second timeout, then clicks it to confirm the cancellation. Uses a try-except block to handle the cancellation feedback page. Attempts to wait for the continue_button to be visible with a 90-second timeout. If the button appears, prints a message indicating the Cancellation Feedback Page is visible, waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5), and if a callback function was provided, calls it with parameters ('Returned_Confirmation_Page', page, screenshot_only=True) to allow custom handling like taking screenshots. If the continue_button does not appear (exception is raised), clicks the confirm_cancellation_button again as a fallback. This method handles the variability in the cancellation flow where a feedback page may or may not appear.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the Terms of Service link opens the correct page in a new popup window. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses page.expect_popup() context manager with a 30-second timeout to capture the popup window that will be opened. Within the context, clicks the terms_and_service_link to trigger the popup. Captures the popup page object in the terms_page variable. Uses expect() to verify that the terms page URL matches the regex pattern r'/terms' with a 90-second timeout, ensuring the popup navigated to the terms of service page. Finally closes the popup window using terms_page.close(). This method validates that the terms link is functional and navigates to the correct destination.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: The plan page count as a string that should appear in the pending change message (e.g., '100')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a pending plan change message is displayed on a banner and contains the expected plan page count. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that the pending_change_plan element is visible with a 90-second timeout. Extracts the actual text content of the banner using update_plan_page.pending_change_plan.inner_text(). Uses regex re.findall(r'\d+', actual_text) to extract all numeric values from the banner text. Asserts that the plan parameter (as a string) is present in the list of extracted numbers, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if the expected plan is not found. This method validates that the UI correctly displays a pending plan change notification with the correct plan details after a user initiates an upgrade or downgrade.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: The button to click on the undo plan modal. Valid values are 'Cancel' or 'Undo Upgrade'.

functions.what_it_does: Handles the undo plan change modal by clicking either the Cancel button to dismiss the modal or the Undo Upgrade button to confirm undoing the plan change. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Clicks the cancel_change_plan_link to open the undo plan change modal. Then checks the button parameter value. If button is 'Cancel', clicks the undo_plan_change_cancel_button with a 90-second timeout, then uses expect() to verify that the cancel button is no longer visible, confirming the modal was dismissed. If button is 'Undo Upgrade', clicks the undo_plan_change_confirmation button with a 90-second timeout, then uses expect() to verify that the confirmation button is no longer visible, confirming the undo action was processed and the modal closed. This method provides a way to test both the cancel and confirm paths of the undo plan change flow.

functions.method_signature: def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price or value as a string (e.g., '9.99')

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as a string (e.g., '100')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information card displays the correct plan value (price) and page count. First creates an UpdatePlanPage instance. Uses expect() to verify that the plan_details_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Extracts the text content of the plan_information element using text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to parse all numeric values from the plan information text. Asserts that the first extracted number (numbers[0]) matches the expected plan_value parameter, raising an AssertionError with a descriptive message if they don't match. Asserts that the second extracted number (numbers[1]) matches the expected plan_pages parameter, raising an AssertionError if they don't match. This method validates that the plan details are correctly displayed after plan selection or when viewing current plan information.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: elements
parameters.description: ElementLocators instance containing selector definitions for the page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the WaitProxy instance by storing references to the Playwright page object and the elements locator container. Sets self.page to the provided page parameter so the proxy can call page.wait_for_selector. Sets self.elements to the provided elements parameter so the proxy can look up selector strings by name. These stored references are used by the __getattr__ method to dynamically create wait functions for element locators.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The attribute name being accessed (should match a locator name)

functions.what_it_does: Intercepts attribute access on the WaitProxy instance to dynamically create wait functions for element locators. When code accesses self.wait.element_name, this method is called with name='element_name'. First checks if the elements object has an attribute with that name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If the attribute exists, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name). Returns a lambda function that accepts keyword arguments and calls self.page.wait_for_selector(selector, **kwargs), allowing the caller to pass timeout and other wait options. If the attribute does not exist on elements, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating no selector with that name was found for wait operations. This dynamic behavior enables clean wait syntax in page objects.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the BasePageObject by storing the Playwright page instance and creating the elements and wait helper objects. Sets self.page to the provided page parameter so all page interaction methods can access the page. Creates self.elements as a new instance of ElementLocators() to serve as a container for selector definitions (subclasses will override this with their own ElementLocators subclass). Creates self.wait as a new WaitProxy instance, passing the page and elements objects, to enable convenient wait operations. This initialization establishes the core infrastructure that all page objects need for element interaction.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The attribute name being accessed (should match a locator name)

functions.what_it_does: Intercepts attribute access on the page object to dynamically convert locator names into Playwright locator objects. When code accesses self.login_button on a page object, this method is called with name='login_button'. First checks if the elements object has an attribute with that name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If the attribute exists, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name) and stores it in locator_str. Then calls self.page.locator(locator_str) to create and return a Playwright locator object for that selector. If the attribute does not exist on elements, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message including the class name. This dynamic behavior allows page objects to access elements using clean attribute syntax like self.login_button.click() instead of self.page.locator(self.elements.login_button).click().

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the selector to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the raw selector string for a given locator name without creating a Playwright locator object. Checks if the elements object has an attribute with the specified name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If the attribute exists, returns the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name). If the attribute does not exist, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message including the class name. This method is useful when code needs the actual selector string rather than a locator object, such as when passing selectors to wait_for_selector or when building composite selectors. While __getattr__ automatically converts locator names to locator objects, this method provides explicit access to the underlying selector strings.

Then defines six locator attributes on the self.elements object for all interactive elements within the cancellation banner component. Sets up see_cancellation_timeline locator using data-testid selector, keep_subscription_button using a composite selector targeting a button within the cancellation banner, cancellation_banner for the main container, resume_button and back_button for the subscription resume modal controls, and resume_banner for the confirmation message. All locators are stored as string selectors on the elements object which is inherited from BasePageObject.

Then systematically defines 78 locator attributes on the self.elements object, organized into logical groups: printer information locators (printer_img, printer_info_name, printer_info_serial), cancellation summary elements (cancellation_summary_image, subscribed_info, confirm_cancellation_button, keep_enrollment_button), retention alternative links (change_plan_link, pause_plan_link, transfer_subscription_link, contact_hp_support_link), change plan modal elements including all plan buttons from 10 to 1500 pages, cancellation feedback survey elements with radio buttons for various cancellation reasons, timeline section locators, paper subscription cancellation elements with paper-specific feedback radio buttons, and various modal and navigation elements. All locators use a mix of data-testid attributes (most reliable), CSS class selectors, and XPath expressions for text-based matching. This comprehensive locator collection supports the entire multi-screen cancellation flow including summary, feedback, retention, and confirmation screens.

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the Playwright locator for a specific plan button based on the provided page count. Constructs the attribute name by formatting plan_attr as 'plan_{plan_pages}_button' (e.g., 'plan_50_button' for plan_pages='50'). Uses hasattr() to check if the self.elements object has an attribute with that name. If the attribute exists, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, plan_attr), creates a Playwright locator from that selector using self.page.locator(selector), and returns the locator object ready for interaction. If the attribute does not exist (invalid plan_pages value), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating the plan button was not found. This method provides a flexible way to select plan buttons without hardcoding the plan value in test code.

Many locators include multiple selector options separated by commas to handle different UI variations. All locators use primarily data-testid attributes with CSS class selectors as alternatives.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the timeline stepper header element to retrieve

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific timeline stepper header element by its index position. First waits for the stepper elements to be present on the page using self.page.wait_for_selector() with the self.elements.stepper selector and a 60 second timeout. Then creates a locator for all stepper elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.stepper) and calls .nth(index) to select the element at the specified zero-based index position. Returns the Playwright locator object for that specific stepper header element, which can then be used for further interactions or assertions. The 60 second timeout ensures the timeline has fully loaded before attempting to access stepper elements.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the CancellationTimelinePage object

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the cancellation step element to retrieve

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds for the step elements to appear and for the specific step to be visible. Defaults to 5000ms (5 seconds)

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves a specific cancellation timeline step element by index with built-in wait logic and error handling. First waits for the cancellation_step elements to be present using self.page.wait_for_selector() with the provided timeout. Creates a locator for all step elements using self.page.locator(self.elements.cancellation_step) and gets the total count of steps using .count(). Validates that the requested index is within the valid range by checking if index is greater than or equal to the count. If the index is out of range, raises an IndexError with a descriptive message showing the requested index and the actual number of steps found. If the index is valid, selects the step at that index using .nth(index), waits for that specific step to be visible using step.wait_for() with state='visible' and the provided timeout, then returns the Playwright locator for that step. This method ensures the step exists and is visible before returning it.

Returns the Playwright locator object for that specific step title, which can be used to get the title text or perform assertions. This is a simple accessor method without built-in wait logic, so the caller should ensure the timeline is loaded before calling this method.

Creates a locator using self.page.locator(self.elements.back_button_choose_plan) which targets all button elements with type='button', then applies a has_text filter with the value 'Back' to narrow down to the specific back button. Returns the filtered locator object which can be used for clicking or verification. This approach is necessary because the base locator '[type="button"]' is too generic and would match multiple buttons, so the text filter ensures the correct button is selected.

Sets up confirmation page elements including terms_agreement_checkbox, enroll_button, billing_card, shipping_card, plan_card with their respective edit buttons and content areas. Includes OOBE-specific elements, FTM redesign elements, FLIP UI elements, header navigation elements, and paper addon elements. This massive initialization ensures all possible UI elements across all enrollment flow variations are accessible for test automation.

parameters.name: parent_element
parameters.description: The parent menu element that needs to be expanded first

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click a submenu target element with automatic parent menu expansion if needed. First tries to click the target_element directly. If the click fails (likely because the submenu is not visible), it catches the exception, clicks the parent_element to expand the parent menu section, and then clicks the target_element again. This handles the case where submenu items are hidden until their parent menu is expanded. The method uses a try-except block to handle the initial click failure gracefully.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: target_element
parameters.description: The Instant Ink submenu element to click

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to click an Instant Ink submenu element with automatic handling of both mobile menu expansion and parent menu expansion. First tries to click the target_element directly with a 2000ms timeout and returns immediately if successful. If that fails, it checks if the mobile menu toggle (expand_side_menu_in_mobile_layout) is visible, and if so, clicks it to expand the side menu in mobile layout, then waits for the target_element to become visible with a 30000ms timeout. Both of these operations are wrapped in try-except blocks to handle cases where they're not needed. Finally, it calls self._click_with_parent_expansion(target_element, self.instant_ink_menu_link) to handle expanding the Instant Ink parent menu if the submenu item is still not clickable. This method handles three levels of menu visibility: mobile menu expansion, parent menu expansion, and the final target click.

It ensures the Account parent menu is expanded before attempting to click the target submenu item.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: The card identifier or index to locate a specific plan card

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright locator for the pages container of a specific ink plan card. Constructs a dynamic selector by interpolating the card parameter into the selector string using an f-string: f"[data-testid='landing-page-plans-container'] [data-testid^='lp-plans-plan-card-{card}'] [class^='styles__PagesContainer-']". This finds the pages container within a specific plan card within the landing page plans container. The method uses page.locator() to create and return the locator object. This is useful for interacting with the page indicators or pagination controls within a specific plan card.

This finds the pages container within a specific plan card identified by the card parameter. The method uses page.locator() to create and return the locator object. This allows test scripts to interact with specific plan cards dynamically based on the card identifier.
