This is useful when there are multiple flag icons on the page but you only need to interact with the first one, typically the one in the header. The @property decorator allows this method to be accessed like an attribute without parentheses.

This property allows test code to access the notifications table element using dot notation like notifications_page.table instead of having to call a method. The returned locator can then be used for interactions like clicking, checking visibility, or extracting text content.

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds to wait for the page title element to appear. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to be present on the page and returns the element locator. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector() method with the self.elements.page_title selector (which is '#rebranded-home-page') and the provided timeout value. This method is useful for verifying that the Overview page has fully loaded before proceeding with other interactions. Returns the Playwright ElementHandle for the page title element once it appears within the timeout period. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will raise a TimeoutError.

parameters.name: self

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the Change Plan link element using Playwright's get_by_test_id() method. Note that the implementation appears to have a bug - it passes self.elements.change_plan_link (which is '[data-testid="change-plan-link"] > span') to get_by_test_id(), but get_by_test_id() expects just the test ID value, not a full selector. The correct usage would be get_by_test_id('change-plan-link'). Returns the first matching element using .first. This method is intended to provide access to the Change Plan link for clicking to navigate to the plan selection page.

parameters.name: self

Returns the first matching element using .first. This method is intended to provide access to the printer name heading element for text extraction or verification.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PlanSelectorV3Page object

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: The plan page value to select, such as '10', '50', '100', '300', etc. This is the number of pages included in the plan.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific subscription plan from the plan selector dropdown by dynamically constructing an XPath selector based on the provided plan_value parameter. The method includes a commented-out CSS selector approach that did not work reliably when the plan value was '10'. Instead, it constructs an XPath selector using the format '//*[@role="option" and contains(normalize-space(.),"{plan_value} ")]' which finds an element with role='option' whose normalized text content contains the plan value followed by a space. Logs the constructed selector using framework_logger.info() for debugging purposes. Then uses self.page.locator() to create a locator with the XPath selector and calls .click() to select that plan option. Finally logs a success message confirming the plan selection. This method handles the dynamic nature of plan options where the exact text format may vary but always includes the page count value.

Then sets a custom default timeout of 60000 milliseconds (60 seconds) on the page using self.page.set_default_timeout(60000) to allow for longer wait times during update configuration operations which may be slower than typical page interactions. Finally defines the continue button locator using data-testid 'btn-continue' and stores it on the self.elements object. The extended timeout is important for this page as printer update operations may involve network calls and processing time.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrinterUpdatesPage object

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to accept automatic printer updates by clicking the continue button on the printer updates page. The method is wrapped in a try-except block for error handling. First tries to click the continue button using self.continue_button.click() which is accessed through the BasePageObject's element access pattern. If the click operation fails for any reason (element not found, not clickable, timeout, etc.), the exception is caught and a descriptive error message is printed to the console using print() with the exception details. The method does not raise the exception, allowing test execution to continue even if the automatic updates acceptance fails. This graceful error handling is useful when the automatic updates page may not always appear in the flow.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrivacyBannerPage object

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to accept the privacy consent banner by clicking the accept button. The method is wrapped in a try-except block for graceful error handling. First tries to click the accept button using self.accept_button.click() which is accessed through the BasePageObject's element access pattern. If the click operation fails for any reason (element not found, not clickable, banner not present, timeout, etc.), the exception is caught and a descriptive error message is printed to the console using print() with the exception details. The method does not raise the exception, allowing test execution to continue even if the privacy banner is not present or cannot be accepted. This graceful error handling is useful because the privacy banner may not appear on every test run (it may only appear on first visit or in certain conditions).

parameters.name: self

parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds to wait for the page title element to appear. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to be present in the DOM and returns the Playwright ElementHandle for that element. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method on the page object with the page_title selector from self.elements and a configurable timeout specified by the wait parameter (defaulting to 60 seconds). parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the FAQ question to retrieve. For example, 0 for the first question, 1 for the second question, etc.

functions.docstring: Returns the locator for a specific FAQ question by index (0-based).
functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright Locator object for a specific FAQ question button based on the provided zero-based index. Uses the page.locator method with the faq_question selector from self.elements (which targets all FAQ accordion buttons), then chains the .nth(index) method to select the specific question at the given index position. This allows tests to interact with individual FAQ questions in the accordion, such as clicking to expand a specific question or verifying its text content. The index parameter is zero-based, so index 0 returns the first FAQ question, index 1 returns the second, and so on.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: String name of the selector to retrieve from the elements dictionary. Must match an existing element name like 'page_title', 'faq_question', 'kCartridgeItem', etc.

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves a selector string from the elements dictionary by name. First checks if the provided name exists as a key in the self.elements dictionary using the 'in' operator. If the name exists, returns the corresponding selector value from self.elements[name]. If the name does not exist in the elements dictionary, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating that the selector name was not found in this page object class. This method provides a safe way to access selectors dynamically with proper error handling, useful when selector names are determined at runtime or passed as parameters.

The billing section includes manage_your_payment_method_link, billing_section container, billing_form_modal, add_payment_method button, address_billing_form_modal, paypal_logo, billing address fields (input_first_address, input_second_address), input_card_number field, error messages (billing_address_error_message, credit_card_invalid_error_message), billing_modal_iframe, billing_next_button, business account fields (business_radio_button, company_name), and card_type_icon. Page-level elements include page_title, cancel_button, update_shipping_billing_message notification, close_modal_button, and continue_button. functions.docstring: Selects the business account type radio button
functions.what_it_does: Selects the business account type radio button on the shipping/billing page. Uses the page.locator method to find the business radio button element using the data-testid selector '[data-testid="business-radio-button"]', then chains .first to get the first matching element (in case multiple exist), and finally calls .click(force=True) to perform a forced click action that bypasses actionability checks. The force=True parameter ensures the click happens even if the element is not in the viewport or is covered by other elements. This is a static method so it can be called without instantiating the ShippingBillingPage class, useful for utility operations or when the page object instance is not available.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) to inherit common page functionality and initialize the self.elements dictionary. Then defines a single UI element locator for the continue button using the ID selector '#full-screen-consent-form-footer-button-continue'. The locator is stored as self.elements.continue_button. This is the simplest page object in the framework with only one locator, reflecting the simple nature of a terms of service consent page that typically has minimal interactive elements.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the UpdatePlanPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines all 60+ locator selectors as attributes on the self.elements object. The locators are organized into logical groups: Plan Details card elements (plan_details_card, replace_printer_link, cancel_instant_ink, cancel_paper_add_on, contact_support_link, change_shipping_link, change_billing_info_link, prepaid_code_link, free_trial_plan_details, paper_free_months, pause_plan_info, resume_plan_link, special_offers_balance, plan_information), Plan Cards for each tier (plan_10 through plan_1500 using container position indices 0-7), Downgrade Plan Modal elements (downgrade_plan_confirmation, cancel_change_plan_button, cancel_change_plan_link, undo_plan_change_cancel_button, undo_plan_change_confirmation, change_plan_button, plan_downgrade_close_modal_button), Upgrade Plan Modal elements (upgrade_plan_confirmation, upgrade_next_month, upgrade_current_month), Pause Plan elements (plan_100_resume_tag, plan_100_resume_button), Header elements (header_cancel_link, header_remove_paper_link), and Page elements (page_title, plan_card_container, free_trial_info_card, disclaimer_card, print_history_link, terms_and_service_link, pending_change_plan), and individual change plan buttons for each tier (change_plan_button_10 through change_plan_button_1500, dashboard_plan_selector_paper_card_button). All locators use data-testid attributes for maximum reliability except for a few using class selectors with starts-with patterns.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: pages
parameters.description: The page count tier for the plan (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, or 1500)

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the change plan button selector for a specific page tier by constructing the attribute name as 'change_plan_button_{pages}' where pages is the provided integer. Uses hasattr() to check if the constructed attribute name exists on the self object. If the attribute exists, returns the selector value using getattr(self, plan_attr). If the attribute does not exist (meaning an invalid page count was provided), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan with the specified page count was not found in UpdatePlanPage. This method enables programmatic access to plan buttons without hardcoding specific page tiers in test code.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: plan_number
parameters.description: The zero-based position index of the plan card (0-7)

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright Locator object for a plan card based on its position index in the plan selector container. Constructs a data-testid selector string dynamically using f-string formatting: f"[data-testid='plans-selector-plan-card-container-{plan_number}']" where plan_number is the provided integer position (0 for 10 pages plan, 1 for 25 pages, 2 for 50 pages, etc.). Calls self.page.locator() with the constructed selector string to create and return a Playwright Locator object that can be used for interactions. This method is useful when the exact page tier is unknown but the position in the UI is known, or when iterating through all plan cards programmatically.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

parameters.name: pages
parameters.description: The page count tier for the plan (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, or 1500)

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the plan card container selector for a specific page tier by constructing the attribute name as 'plan_{pages}' where pages is the provided integer. Uses hasattr() to check if the constructed attribute name exists on the self object. If the attribute exists, returns the selector value using getattr(self, plan_attr). If the attribute does not exist (meaning an invalid page count was provided), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan with the specified page count was not found in UpdatePlanPage. This method enables programmatic access to plan card containers by page tier without hardcoding specific values in test code.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the cancel Instant Ink link. Accesses the cancel_instant_ink selector from self.elements.cancel_instant_ink and passes it to self.page.locator() to create a Locator. Uses the .last property on the Locator to select the last matching element on the page, which is useful when multiple cancel links exist and the last one is the relevant one (possibly due to DOM structure or multiple instances). Returns the Locator object that can be used for click, visibility checks, or other interactions. This property provides a convenient way to access the cancel link without calling a method.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the free trial information card. Accesses the free_trial_info_card selector from self.elements.free_trial_info_card and passes it to self.page.locator() to create a Locator. Uses the .first property on the Locator to select the first matching element on the page, which is useful when multiple trial info cards might exist and the first one is the primary one to interact with. Returns the Locator object that can be used for visibility checks, text extraction, or other interactions. This property provides a convenient way to access the trial info card without calling a method.

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrinterData instance by setting named attributes for all six printer data fields. Assigns self.entity_id, self.model_number, self.device_uuid, self.cloud_id, self.postcard, and self.fingerprint from the provided parameters. This allows accessing printer data using both tuple indexing (printer[0]) and named attributes (printer.entity_id).
functions.usage_example: printer = PrinterData('ABC123', '1G5L3A', 'uuid-123', 'cloud-456', 'postcard-data', 'fingerprint-data'); entity = printer.entity_id; model = printer[1]

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Dictionary containing all configuration parameters including stack, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, tenant_country, tenant_country_short, tenant_country_language_code, tenant_address, headless, target, simulator_platform, target_width, target_height, supplyvariant, card_payment_gateway, payment_method

functions.what_it_does: Sets all global module-level configuration variables from the provided args dictionary. Extracts stack, profile, derivative_model, biz_model, tenant country information, display settings, and payment configuration. Then loads the corresponding stack configuration from STACK_CONFIG dictionary to set all environment-specific URLs (instantink_url, agena_url, cartridgepacker_url, fulfillmentservice_url, oss_url, hpid_url, stratus endpoints, ucde endpoints) and client secrets. Also initializes MongoDB and S3 storage configuration. Logs all loaded configuration values for debugging. This function must be called before any test execution to configure the framework for the target environment.

Initializes the test framework by calling set_globals() with configuration values extracted from GlobalState. Reads stack, printer_profile, derivative_model, biz_model, supplyvariant, country, country_code, language_code, address_payload, headless, target, simulator_platform, card_payment_gateway, and payment_method from GlobalState and passes them to set_globals(). Then creates a 'temp' directory for temporary file storage during test execution. This is typically called in conftest.py or test setup fixtures to initialize the framework before test execution.

Generates a unique email address for test tenant creation by appending a random 8-character alphanumeric suffix to 'hello.instantink+' and '@gmail.com'. Uses random.choices() to generate the suffix from lowercase letters and digits. Stores the generated email in the global _tenant_email variable and returns it. The plus-addressing format (hello.instantink+suffix@gmail.com) allows all emails to be delivered to the same Gmail inbox while maintaining unique addresses for each test execution.

Authenticates with Gmail API using OAuth2 refresh token stored in environment variables. Creates a Credentials object with GMAIL_REFRESH_TOKEN, GMAIL_CLIENT_ID, and GMAIL_CLIENT_SECRET. Calls creds.refresh() to obtain a new access token using the refresh token. If refresh fails with invalid_grant or invalid_request error, raises RuntimeError indicating the refresh token needs to be updated. Returns a Gmail API service object built using the refreshed credentials. This service object is used by fetch_verification_code() to read verification emails.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML content of the verification email

functions.what_it_does: Extracts the 6-digit verification code from HPID verification email HTML content. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse the HTML and finds a paragraph element with class 'code'. Extracts the text content from this element using get_text(strip=True) and returns it. If the code element is not found, raises RuntimeError. This is the original extraction method that looks for a specific HTML structure. The newer new_extract_verification_code() function provides more robust extraction with regex fallbacks.

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address to search for verification code

functions.what_it_does: Fetches the HPID verification code from Gmail for the specified tenant email address. Authenticates with Gmail API using authenticate_gmail(). Searches for messages sent to the tenant email within the last 5 minutes using query 'to:{email} newer_than:5m'. Retrieves the most recent message in full format. Extracts the HTML body from the message payload (either from main body or from parts array). Decodes the base64-encoded HTML content. Calls new_extract_verification_code() to extract the 6-digit code from the HTML. Returns the verification code string. The @retry decorator automatically retries up to 3 times with 5-second delays if the function fails (e.g., if no messages are found yet).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute after submitting signup form

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address to use for signup. If None, uses global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new HPID account through the HP ID signup page. Navigates to the HPID URL (_hpid_url). Sets viewport to 1366x768. Waits for and clicks the sign-up button. Fills the signup form with DEFAULT_FIRSTNAME, DEFAULT_LASTNAME, provided email (or _tenant_email), and DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks the sign-up submit button. Waits for page load. Executes the optional callback function if provided. Then enters a 60-second polling loop checking if either the OTP code entry screen (#submit-code) or the myaccount page has loaded. If OTP screen appears, calls confirm_code_by_onboarding_center(). If myaccount page loads, calls confirm_code_by_my_account(). Returns the page object. This function handles HPID creation for older enrollment flows.

parameters.name: check_func
parameters.description: Lambda or function to execute for checking a condition

functions.what_it_does: Safely executes a check function and returns its result, catching any exceptions and returning False if an exception occurs. This is used in polling loops to check for element presence or page conditions without crashing if the element doesn't exist yet. Wraps the check_func call in a try-except block that returns False on any exception.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Confirms the HPID verification code through the My Account security page. Clicks the security tab (li[id='security']). Clicks the Verify button. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the 6-digit code from email. Fills the code into the textbox with name 'Enter 6-digit numeric code'. Clicks the Verify button to submit the code. This flow is used when HPID signup redirects to the My Account page for email verification.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Confirms the HPID verification code through the Onboarding Center OTP screen. Calls get_verification_code() to retrieve the 6-digit code from email. Fills the code into the #code input field. Clicks the #submit-code button. Then attempts to handle the country residence selector if it appears (waits up to 20 seconds for #countryResidence). If the selector appears, clicks it, selects the country using the _tenant_country_short code from a data-value attribute, and clicks the continue button. If the country selector doesn't appear within 20 seconds, logs a debug message and continues. Logs success message when HPID is created. This flow is used when HPID signup shows the OTP entry screen directly.

Retrieves the HPID verification code from email with retry logic. Attempts to call fetch_verification_code(_tenant_email) up to 3 times. If fetch_verification_code() raises an exception (typically because no email has arrived yet), logs a warning and waits 15 seconds before retrying. If all 3 attempts fail, raises RuntimeError. If successful, logs the retrieved code and returns it. This function provides a higher-level retry wrapper around fetch_verification_code() with longer delays between attempts.
functions.usage_example: code = get_verification_code(); # Returns '123456' after retrying if needed

parameters.name: test_requirements
parameters.description: Dictionary containing test requirements including optional plan_pages key

functions.docstring: Determine plan pages from requirements or use default.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan page count to use for enrollment testing. Checks if test_requirements dictionary contains a 'plan_pages' key. If present, returns that value and logs it. If not present, returns the default value '100' and logs that the default is being used. This allows tests to specify custom plan page counts or fall back to a standard default.

parameters.name: plan_type_param
parameters.description: Plan type parameter passed to the function

parameters.name: test_requirements
parameters.description: Dictionary containing test requirements including optional paper_option key

functions.docstring: Determine plan type from parameter or requirements.
functions.what_it_does: Determines the plan type (ink_only or ink_and_paper) for enrollment. If plan_type_param is not 'ink_only', returns plan_type_param directly and logs it. If plan_type_param is 'ink_only', checks the paper_option key in test_requirements. If paper_option is 'add', returns 'ink_and_paper'. Otherwise returns 'ink_only'. Logs the determined plan type with the paper_option value. This allows tests to specify plan type directly or derive it from paper option requirements.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account through the enrollment flow. First tests browser connectivity by navigating to about:blank. Then navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait and 120-second timeout. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Attempts to accept privacy banner if present. Locates and clicks the sign-up button (tries multiple test IDs). Clicks the create account button. Waits for the sign-in form to appear. Waits explicitly for firstName field to be visible and interactive. Fills firstName, lastName, email (_tenant_email), and password (DEFAULT_PASSWORD). Clicks the market checkbox. Clicks sign-up submit. Waits for OTP code entry screen. Retrieves verification code with retry logic (up to 3 attempts with 5-second delays). Fills the code and submits. Waits for the stepper element to appear indicating successful account creation. Navigates to portalshell URL and waits 60 seconds. Attempts to click side menu toggle. Clicks the HP Instant Ink menu item. Returns the page object. This is the primary account creation flow for v2 enrollment tests.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username/email to login with

parameters.name: oobe
parameters.description: Boolean flag indicating if this is an OOBE (out-of-box-experience) login flow

functions.what_it_does: Logs into an existing Instant Ink v2 account. Checks if sign-up button is visible (timeout 5 seconds). If not visible, navigates to _instantink_url. Attempts to accept privacy banner if present. Clicks the sign-up button. Waits 5 seconds. Clicks the sign-in button. Fills username field. Clicks 'Use password' link. Fills password field with DEFAULT_PASSWORD. Clicks sign-in button. If oobe parameter is True, waits for device selection radio button to appear (up to 180 seconds), clicks the 'Add a different printer' radio button, waits for continue button, and clicks it. Returns the page object. This function is used to login to existing accounts for testing scenarios that require pre-existing users.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Logs out of the Instant Ink v2 account. Locates the account button using data-testid 'account-button'. Expects it to be visible within 3 seconds. Clicks the account button to open the account menu. Locates the sign-out button using data-testid 'sign-out-button'. Expects it to be visible within 3 seconds. Clicks the sign-out button. Waits for the sign-up button to appear (up to 120 seconds) to confirm logout was successful. This function is used in test teardown or when testing multiple user scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Onboards an HPID user to the UCDE (Unified Cloud Device Ecosystem) system via OAuth flow. Generates a random GUID for the OAuth state parameter. Constructs the UCDE authorization URL with openid, email, and profile scopes, using _ucde_client_id and REDIRECT_URI. Navigates the page to the authorization URL. Waits for page load. Waits 5 seconds for redirect. Extracts the authorization code from the final redirect URI. Exchanges the authorization code for access_token and id_token by POSTing to the UCDE token endpoint with Basic authentication (client_id:client_secret base64 encoded). Validates that both tokens were received. Creates a UCDE account by POSTing to the accounts endpoint with the id_token, country set, and language. Logs the response. Returns the page object. This function is critical for enabling UCDE-based device management and enrollment flows.

Obtains an OAuth access token for the Gemini client using client credentials grant. Constructs the token URL from _stratus_authz. Creates Basic authentication header by base64 encoding gemini_client_id:gemini_client_secret. POSTs to the token endpoint with grant_type=client_credentials. Raises exception if request fails. Extracts and returns the access_token from the JSON response. This token is used for Gemini API calls that require service-level authentication.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the user. If None, uses global _tenant_email

functions.what_it_does: Obtains an organization-aware user access token for the specified email address through a multi-step token exchange process. Step 1: Gets Gemini client token using get_token_gemini_client(). Step 2: Uses Gemini token to query usermgtsvc for user details by email, extracts userResourceId and idpId. Step 3: Uses Gemini token to query usertenantdetails by userResourceId, extracts tenantResourceId. Step 4: Gets Stratus client token using client credentials grant with stratus_client_id and stratus_client_secret. Step 5: Performs token exchange by POSTing to token endpoint with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange, subject_token=stratus_client_token, requested_user=userResourceId, and tenant_id=tenantResourceId. Extracts the final org-aware access token from the response. Returns a tuple of (org_token, tenant_id). This token has user context and tenant context, allowing API calls to act on behalf of the user within their tenant. Used for device claiming, address management, and subscription operations.
functions.usage_example: org_token, tenant_id = get_org_aware_token('test@example.com'); # Use org_token for API calls

functions.method_signature: def retry_operation(operation, operation_name="Operation", max_attempts=5, delay=2, on_retry=None)

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Function to execute

parameters.name: operation_name
parameters.description: Name of the operation for logging

parameters.name: max_attempts
parameters.description: Maximum number of retry attempts

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Delay in seconds between retries

parameters.name: on_retry
parameters.description: Optional callback function to execute before each retry

functions.what_it_does: Generic retry wrapper that executes an operation function with automatic retry logic. Attempts to call the operation function up to max_attempts times. Logs each attempt number. If the operation succeeds, logs success and returns the result. If the operation raises an exception, logs the error. If this was the last attempt, logs failure and re-raises the exception. If there are attempts remaining, optionally calls the on_retry callback function (catching and logging any errors from the callback). Waits for the specified delay before the next attempt. This utility is used throughout the module to add retry logic to flaky operations like API calls and printer creation.
functions.usage_example: result = retry_operation(lambda: api_call(), operation_name='API Call', max_attempts=3, delay=5)

functions.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer using the G2 simulator API through a multi-step process with retry logic. Defines a helper function make_request() for HTTP requests. Step 1: POSTs to the simulator printers endpoint with stack, profile, biz_model, offer, fipsflag, and derivative_model to create the printer. Uses retry_operation() with 5 attempts. Extracts entity_id (serial number), model_number, and device_uuid from response. Step 2: POSTs to the register endpoint for the created printer to register it with the cloud. Uses retry_operation() with 5 attempts. Extracts cloud_id from response. Step 3: POSTs to the claimpostcard endpoint to get the claim postcard data. Uses retry_operation() with 3 attempts. Base64 encodes the postcard if it's JSON, otherwise uses it as-is. Step 4: GETs the devicefingerprint endpoint to get the device fingerprint. Uses retry_operation() with 3 attempts. Base64 encodes the fingerprint if it's JSON, otherwise uses it as-is. Creates a PrinterData tuple with all six values. Appends it to the global _printer_created list. Returns the PrinterData object. This function is critical for creating test printers for enrollment and device management testing.
functions.usage_example: printer = create_virtual_printer(); entity_id = printer.entity_id; cloud_id = printer.cloud_id

functions.method_signature: def claim_virtual_printer(org_token, tenant_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, fingerprint)

parameters.name: org_token
parameters.description: Organization-aware access token

parameters.name: tenant_id
parameters.description: Tenant resource ID

parameters.name: model_number
parameters.description: Printer model number

parameters.name: device_uuid
parameters.description: Device UUID

parameters.name: postcard
parameters.description: Claim postcard data

parameters.name: fingerprint
parameters.description: Device fingerprint data

functions.what_it_does: Claims a virtual printer to a tenant account using the UCDE API with retry logic. Constructs the autoClaimRequest payload with productNumber, uuid, claimPostcard, fingerprint, and selectedBizModel. Attempts to POST to the UCDE devices endpoint under the tenant account up to 10 times. Uses the org_token for Bearer authentication. If the request succeeds (status 200), logs success and returns. If the request fails, logs the error. If this was the last attempt, logs failure and re-raises the exception. Otherwise waits 2 seconds and retries. This function associates the virtual printer with the tenant account, making it available for enrollment.

parameters.name: org_token
parameters.description: Organization-aware access token

parameters.name: tenant_id
parameters.description: Tenant resource ID

functions.what_it_does: Adds a shipping address to the tenant account using the Gemini comfe API. Checks if _tenant_address is a list and extracts the first element if so, otherwise uses it directly. Filters out any keys starting with 'fullState' from the address data. POSTs the address payload to the comfe shipping addresses endpoint with tenantId query parameter. Uses org_token for Bearer authentication. Checks if response status is 201 (Created). If not 201, raises RuntimeError. Logs success message. This function is used to pre-populate shipping addresses for enrollment testing.

Creates and claims a virtual printer in one operation. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org_token and tenant_id. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer and get all printer data. Unpacks the PrinterData tuple into entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint. Logs all printer details. Calls claim_virtual_printer() with the org_token, tenant_id, and printer data to claim the printer to the tenant. Logs success. Waits 600 seconds (10 minutes) as a temporary fix for printer availability issues (references bug tickets III-2131 and IIGAR-2774). Returns the PrinterData object. This is a convenience function that combines printer creation and claiming for test setup.

Creates and claims a virtual printer and adds a shipping address in one operation. Calls get_org_aware_token() to obtain org_token and tenant_id. Calls create_virtual_printer() to create the printer. Unpacks the PrinterData tuple. Logs printer details. Calls claim_virtual_printer() to claim the printer. Calls add_address_to_tenant() to add the shipping address using the comfe API. Logs that address was added successfully. Logs that it's waiting 10 minutes for printer availability. Waits 600 seconds as a temporary fix for printer availability issues. Returns the PrinterData object. This is a convenience function that combines printer creation, claiming, and address setup for enrollment tests that require all three.

parameters.name: payment_type
parameters.description: Payment method type key. If None, uses global _payment_method or defaults to 'credit_card_master'

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves payment method test data from payment_method_data.json file. If payment_type is None, uses the global _payment_method variable or defaults to 'credit_card_master'. Loads the JSON file using load_json_file(). Validates that the payment_type exists in the data, raising ValueError with available types if not found. Creates a copy of the payment data for the specified type. Checks if the data contains an 'expiration_year' field. If the expiration_year value is a string starting with '+', calculates the actual year by adding the number after '+' to the current year. For example, '+6' becomes current_year + 6. Returns the payment data dictionary with the calculated expiration year. This function is used by payment page objects to get test payment credentials.

parameters.name: org_token
parameters.description: Organization-aware access token

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Printer serial number / entity ID

parameters.name: model_number
parameters.description: Printer model number

functions.what_it_does: Creates an OOBE (Out-Of-Box Experience) registration record in Agena for the specified printer. Constructs a payload with model_number, serial_number (entity_id), country (_tenant_country_short), and empty strings for sku, ink_declaration, supply_types, supply_levels, supply_states, unenrolled_pages, ui_phase, enrollment_kit, jump_id, and ip_address. POSTs to the Agena oobe_registrations endpoint with Bearer authentication using org_token and Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=2'. Logs the request and response. Raises exception if request fails. Returns the response object. This function simulates the OOBE registration that normally happens when a printer is first powered on.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of the card header to find

functions.what_it_does: Finds a card body element by locating its header text. Locates elements with class 'card-header' that have exact text matching header_text using :text-is() selector. Asserts that at least one matching header exists. Navigates up one level from the header using '..' to get the card container. Then locates the '.card-body' element within that container. Returns the card body locator. This is used for extracting data from card-based UI layouts in admin interfaces.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of the card header

functions.what_it_does: Extracts text content from a card body by finding the card header. Calls find_card_body_by_header() to locate the card body element. Calls inner_text() on the card body locator to get all text content. Strips leading and trailing whitespace. Returns the cleaned text string. This is used for extracting field values from card-based admin UI layouts.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: header_text
parameters.description: Exact text of the card header

functions.what_it_does: Extracts a list of link URLs from a card body by finding the card header. Calls find_card_body_by_header() to locate the card body element. Locates all 'a' (anchor) elements within the card body. Iterates through all link elements using .all(). For each link, extracts the href attribute using get_attribute('href'). Returns a list of all href values. This is used for extracting multiple links from card-based admin UI layouts.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Extracts the last number from the current page URL. Gets the current URL from page.url. Uses regex re.findall(r'\d+', url) to find all sequences of digits in the URL. Returns the last number from the list if any numbers were found, otherwise returns None. This is commonly used to extract IDs from URLs like '/subscriptions/12345' where 12345 is the subscription ID.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text string to extract numbers from

functions.what_it_does: Extracts all numbers (integers and decimals) from a text string. Returns empty list if text is None or empty. Strips whitespace from the text. Uses regex re.findall(r'\d+\.\d+|\d+', text) to find all decimal numbers (e.g., '12.34') and integers (e.g., '56'). Returns the list of matched number strings. Returns empty list if no matches found. This is used for extracting numeric values from UI text that may contain mixed content.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Printer serial number / entity ID

parameters.name: cloud_id
parameters.description: Cloud ID

parameters.name: device_uuid
parameters.description: Device UUID

functions.what_it_does: Sends an RTP (Real-Time Printer) device status message to the simulator to trigger supply level updates and impression count reporting. Initializes counter values (bizmodelFlag, lastValidConfigSeqNum, lastReceivedConfigSeqNum, lastValidCredentialSeqNum, supplyInsertionCount, totalImpressionCount, bizlogicImpressionCount, internalReportImpressionCount, snapshotCountersSum). Generates a unique timestamp. Starts with a base64-encoded crsSessionData template. Decodes the template into a mutable bytearray. Uses struct.pack to write the counter values and device_uuid into specific byte offsets in the binary data. Re-encodes the modified data to base64. Defines a decode_crssession function to verify the packed data. Logs the decoded values for debugging. Creates a replacements dictionary with all values including cloud_id, uuid, crsSessionData, counters, and timestamp. Loads the RTP payload template JSON file based on _supplyvariant (RTPDS-PayLoad-IIC.json or RTPDS-PayLoad-IPH.json). Replaces all {{placeholder}} values in the template with actual values. Parses the template as JSON. Recursively coerces numeric string values to integers for specific keys. Logs the final payload. PUTs the payload to the simulator rtpdevicestatustriggers endpoint. Logs and returns the response. This function simulates printer status reporting to trigger supply shipments and subscription state changes.

parameters.name: identifier_code
parameters.description: Offer identifier code from offers.json

functions.what_it_does: Generates an offer code (POSA code) from an offer identifier by calling the Agena codes API. Gets an org-aware token using get_org_aware_token(). Constructs the Agena codes URL with the identifier_code. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=1', and Content-Type 'application/json'. POSTs to the codes endpoint. Logs the response. Raises exception if request fails. Extracts the code from response JSON at path ['posaCodes'][0]['code']. Logs and returns the generated code. This code can be used during enrollment to apply the offer.
functions.usage_example: code = offer_request('10232'); # Returns a POSA code like 'ABC123DEF456'

functions.method_signature: def get_offer(region: str, type: str = "free", amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> dict

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Country code (e.g., 'US', 'DE', 'CA')

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Offer type: 'free' or 'prepaid'

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for offers with exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount greater than this value in cents

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount less than this value in cents

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves an offer from the OFFERS_DATA (loaded from offers.json) based on region and filtering criteria. Validates that region is provided, raising ValueError if not. Validates that region exists in OFFERS_DATA, raising ValueError if not found. Gets the list of offers for the region. Iterates through offers checking if the type matches. If no amount filters are provided, returns the first matching offer. If amount filters are provided, validates that the offer has an amountCents field and converts it to int. Checks the amount against amount_equal (must equal), amount_greater (must be greater than), and amount_less (must be less than) filters. Returns the first offer that matches all criteria. If no matching offer is found, constructs an error message listing the filters that were applied and raises ValueError. This function is used to select appropriate offers for enrollment tests based on test requirements.
functions.usage_example: offer = get_offer('US', type='prepaid', amount_equal=6000); guid = offer['guid']; identifier = offer['identifier']

functions.method_signature: def get_offer_code(offer_type: str = "free", amount_equal: int = None, amount_greater: int = None, amount_less: int = None) -> str

parameters.name: offer_type
parameters.description: Offer type: 'free' or 'prepaid'

parameters.name: amount_equal
parameters.description: Filter for offers with exact amount in cents

parameters.name: amount_greater
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount greater than this value

parameters.name: amount_less
parameters.description: Filter for offers with amount less than this value

functions.what_it_does: Generates an offer code for the current test country based on offer type and amount filters. Gets the country code from GlobalState.country_code. Validates that country_code is set, raising ValueError if not. Calls get_offer() with the region, offer_type, and amount filters to find a matching offer. Extracts the identifier from the offer. Validates that identifier exists, raising ValueError if not. Calls offer_request() with the identifier to generate the POSA code. Logs and returns the generated code. This is a convenience function that combines offer selection and code generation for test setup.
functions.usage_example: code = get_offer_code(offer_type='prepaid', amount_equal=6000); # Returns POSA code for $60 prepaid offer

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Captures user information by intercepting the /v3/usermgtsvc/users/me API request on the dashboard page. Logs that it's waiting for the request. Uses page.expect_response() with pattern '**/v3/usermgtsvc/users/me' and 120-second timeout to wait for the request. Reloads the page to trigger the request. When the response is captured, extracts the response object and its request. Attempts to parse the response body as JSON, catching any errors and storing an error message if parsing fails. Constructs a captured_data dictionary containing the request (method, url, headers, body) and response (status, status_text, headers, data). Prints and returns the captured_data. If the request is not captured within the timeout, logs a warning and returns None. This function is used to extract authentication tokens and user data from dashboard API calls.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves user information by calling the Gemini /api/users/me endpoint using a token captured from the dashboard. Calls user_info_from_dashboard() to capture the API request and extract the authorization token. Removes 'Bearer ' prefix from the token. Constructs the Gemini users/me URL. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept and Content-Type as application/json. GETs the endpoint using the session. Logs the response. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data. This function provides detailed user and subscription information.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Subscription ID

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves detailed subscription data from Gemini API for a specific subscription ID. Gets a Gemini client token using get_token_gemini_client(). Constructs the Gemini subscriptions URL with the subscription ID. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept and Content-Type as application/json. GETs the endpoint. Logs the response. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing full subscription details. This function is used for subscription state validation and data extraction.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Subscription ID

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the subscription state for a specific subscription ID. Calls subscription_data_from_gemini() to get full subscription data. Attempts to extract the subscriptionState from the nested JSON path ['userSubInfo']['subscriptionInfo']['minSubInfo']['subscriptionState']. Returns the subscription state string if found. If a KeyError occurs (path doesn't exist), returns None. This function is used for subscription state validation in tests.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the subscription state by calling the /api/users/me endpoint via the dashboard. Calls request_me_by_dashboard() to get user data. Attempts to extract the subscriptionState from the nested JSON path ['userMinSubInfo']['minSubInfo'][0]['subscriptionState']. Returns the subscription state string if found. If a KeyError occurs, returns None. This function provides an alternative way to get subscription state using the dashboard API.

parameters.name: sub_id
parameters.description: Subscription ID

parameters.name: expected_state
parameters.description: Expected subscription state string

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a subscription reaches the expected state within 60 seconds. Records the start time. Enters a polling loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. In each iteration, calls get_subscription_state() to get the current state. If the state matches expected_state, logs success and breaks out of the loop. Otherwise waits 1 second and checks again. After the loop, asserts that the final state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError with a descriptive message if not. This function is used in tests to wait for subscription state transitions after actions like enrollment, suspension, or cancellation.
functions.usage_example: validate_subscription_state('12345', 'ACTIVE'); # Waits up to 60s for subscription to become ACTIVE

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: expected_state
parameters.description: Expected subscription state string

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a subscription reaches the expected state within 60 seconds using the dashboard API. Records the start time. Enters a polling loop that runs for up to 60 seconds. In each iteration, calls get_subscription_state_by_me_from_dashboard() to get the current state. If the state matches expected_state, logs success and breaks out of the loop. Otherwise waits 1 second and checks again. After the loop, asserts that the final state equals expected_state, raising AssertionError with a descriptive message if not. This provides an alternative validation method using the dashboard API instead of direct Gemini API calls.
functions.usage_example: validate_subscription_state_by_me(page, 'ACTIVE'); # Waits up to 60s for subscription to become ACTIVE

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Printer serial number / entity ID

functions.what_it_does: Removes web services from a virtual printer by calling the simulator API. Logs that it's removing web services for the entity_id. Constructs the simulator webservices URL for the printer. Sends a DELETE request to the endpoint. Logs the response status and text. Checks if status is 200. If not 200, raises RuntimeError. Logs success message. This function simulates disabling web services on a printer, which can be used to test offline scenarios or web services re-enablement flows.

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Printer serial number / entity ID

functions.what_it_does: Enables web services on a virtual printer by calling the simulator API. Logs that it's enabling web services for the entity_id. Constructs the simulator webservices URL for the printer. Sends a PUT request to the endpoint. Logs the response status and text. Checks if status is 200. If not 200, raises RuntimeError. Logs success message. This function simulates enabling web services on a printer, which is required for printer connectivity and enrollment.

Returns the list of cartridge colors for the current printer based on its supply variant. Checks the global _supplyvariant variable. If _supplyvariant is 'IPH' (Instant Ink Photo), returns ['CMY', 'K'] indicating a combined color cartridge and separate black cartridge. If _supplyvariant is 'IIC' (Instant Ink Cartridge), returns ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] indicating four separate cartridges for black, cyan, magenta, and yellow. This function is used for supply level validation and cartridge-related test assertions.
functions.usage_example: colors = printer_colors(); # Returns ['CMY', 'K'] for IPH or ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y'] for IIC

parameters.name: date_string
parameters.description: Date string in various formats

functions.what_it_does: Parses a date string in multiple possible formats and returns a datetime object. Defines a list of date format strings including: '%b %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM), '%Y/%m/%d %I:%M:%S %p' (2025/09/14 02:41:13 PM), '%d %b %Y, %H:%M:%S' (14 Sep 2025, 14:41:13), '%d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S' (14/09/2025 14:41:13), '%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p' (09/14/2025 2:41:11 PM), '%B %d, %Y, %I:%M:%S %p' (September 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM). Iterates through each format attempting to parse the date_string using datetime.strptime(). If parsing succeeds, returns the datetime object. If parsing fails with ValueError, continues to the next format. If all formats fail, raises ValueError with the original date_string. This function handles date parsing for various locale-specific date formats found in the UI.
functions.usage_example: date_obj = parse_flexible_date('Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM'); # Returns datetime object

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text containing a date string

functions.what_it_does: Extracts a date from text that may contain other content. Cleans the text by replacing multiple whitespace characters with single spaces and stripping leading/trailing whitespace. Defines a list of regex patterns to match various date formats. Iterates through each pattern attempting to find a match using re.search(). If a match is found, extracts the date string from the first capture group. Logs the extracted date string. Calls parse_flexible_date() to parse the date string into a datetime object. Returns the datetime object. If no pattern matches, raises ValueError with the cleaned text. This function is used to extract dates from UI text that may contain labels or other content.
functions.usage_example: date_obj = extract_date_from_text('Next shipment: Sep 14, 2025, 2:41:11 PM'); # Returns datetime object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves all available Instant Ink plans for the current country from the Agena API. Constructs the Agena plans URL with country parameter (_tenant_country_short), program parameter 'i_ink', and obsolete parameter 'false'. Gets a Gemini client token. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=3', and Content-Type 'application/json'. GETs the plans endpoint. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing the list of available plans. This function is used to retrieve plan information for plan selection and validation.
functions.usage_example: plans = get_plans_data(); # Returns list of plan dictionaries

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Plan attribute key to filter by

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to match for the specified key

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Calls get_plans_data() to get all plans. Converts the value parameter to int. Filters the plans list to include only plans where the specified key equals the value. If no matching plans are found, raises ValueError with the key and value. Returns the list of matching plans. This function is used to find plans with specific characteristics like page count.
functions.usage_example: plans_100 = get_filtered_plan_data(key='pages', value=100); # Returns all 100-page plans

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves all available yearly Instant Ink plans for the current country from the Agena API. Constructs the Agena plans URL with country parameter (_tenant_country_short), program parameter 'i_yearly_ink', and obsolete parameter 'false'. Gets a Gemini client token. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=3', and Content-Type 'application/json'. GETs the plans endpoint. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing the list of available yearly plans. This function is used to retrieve yearly plan information for annual enrollment testing.
functions.usage_example: yearly_plans = get_yearly_plans_data(); # Returns list of yearly plan dictionaries

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Plan attribute key to filter by

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value to match for the specified key

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves yearly plans filtered by a specific attribute value. Calls get_yearly_plans_data() to get all yearly plans. Converts the value parameter to int. Filters the plans list to include only plans where the specified key equals the value. If no matching plans are found, raises ValueError with the key and value. Returns the list of matching plans. This function is used to find yearly plans with specific characteristics like annual page count.
functions.usage_example: yearly_plans_1200 = get_filtered_yearly_plan_data(key='pages', value=1200); # Returns all 1200-page yearly plans

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected billing status code based on the current payment method. Checks if 'credit_card' is in the global _payment_method string. If yes, sets status to 'CPT-100'. If the _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides status to 'STRATUS-PAYMENT-COMPLETE'. Checks if 'direct_debit' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets status to 'DIRECT-DEBIT-SUCCESS'. Checks if 'paypal' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets status to 'PAY-PAL-SUCCESS'. Checks if 'prepaid' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets status to 'PEGASUS-SUCCESS'. Returns the determined status code. This function is used for payment status validation in tests.
functions.usage_example: expected_status = billing_status_code(); # Returns 'CPT-100' for credit card with PGS gateway

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Boolean indicating if this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund event response status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines a nested dictionary mapping payment method types to full and partial refund status codes. Normalizes the _payment_method by extracting the base type (credit_card, direct_debit, paypal, or prepaid_only). Gets the refund status codes for the normalized payment method. If full parameter is True, returns the full refund status code. Otherwise returns the partial refund status code. This function is used for refund event validation in tests.
functions.usage_example: full_refund_status = request_event_response_status(full=True); # Returns 'PAYMENT-GATEWAY-REFUNDED' for credit card

parameters.name: full
parameters.description: Boolean indicating if this is a full refund (True) or partial refund (False)

functions.what_it_does: Returns the expected refund status code based on payment method and refund type. Defines a nested dictionary mapping payment method types to full and partial refund status codes. Normalizes the _payment_method by extracting the base type (credit_card, direct_debit, paypal, or prepaid_only). Gets the refund status codes for the normalized payment method. If full parameter is True, returns the full refund status code. Otherwise returns the partial refund status code. This function is used for refund status validation in tests.
functions.usage_example: full_refund = refund_status(full=True); # Returns 'FULL-REFUND' for credit card

functions.what_it_does: Returns the payment engine name based on the current payment method. Checks if 'credit_card' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'payment_gateway'. If _card_payment_gateway is '2CO', overrides to 'stratus'. Checks if 'direct_debit' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'pgs_direct_debit'. Checks if 'paypal' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'pgs_pay_pal'. Checks if 'prepaid_only' is in _payment_method. If yes, sets payment_engine to 'pegasus'. Returns the determined payment engine name. This function is used for payment engine validation and routing in tests.
functions.usage_example: engine = payment_engine(); # Returns 'payment_gateway' for credit card with PGS

functions.what_it_does: Returns a list of country names that do not require state/province selection in address forms. Returns a hardcoded list containing: Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Luxembourg, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Singapore, Sweden, Switzerland. This function is used to determine whether state/province field validation should be applied in address entry tests.
functions.usage_example: no_state_countries = countries_without_states_mandate(); if country in no_state_countries: skip_state_validation()

functions.what_it_does: Returns a list of country names that support direct debit payment method. Returns a hardcoded list containing: Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Spain. This function is used to determine whether direct debit payment option should be available in payment method selection tests.
functions.usage_example: direct_debit_countries = countries_with_direct_debit(); if country in direct_debit_countries: test_direct_debit_option()

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: locator
parameters.description: Playwright Locator object

functions.what_it_does: Checks if an element is actionable (clickable and not obscured by other elements). Gets the bounding box of the element using locator.bounding_box(). Returns False if no bounding box is found. Calculates the center point of the element (center_x, center_y). Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that calls document.elementFromPoint() at the center coordinates. Checks if the element at that point is either the target element itself or contains the target element. Returns True if the element is actionable, False otherwise. Catches any exceptions and returns False. This function is used to verify that elements are truly clickable before attempting interactions.

parameters.name: json_string
parameters.description: JSON string to parse

functions.what_it_does: Parses a JSON string and returns the parsed object. Attempts to parse the json_string using json.loads(). If parsing fails, logs an error with the exception details and re-raises the exception. This is a simple wrapper around json.loads() that adds error logging.

Retrieves printer SKU data from the Agena API for the current country and derivative model. Constructs the Agena printer_skus URL with _tenant_country_short and _derivative_model parameters. Gets a Gemini client token. Sets headers with Bearer authentication, Accept header 'application/vnd.agena+json;version=3', and Content-Type 'application/json'. GETs the printer_skus endpoint. Raises exception if request fails. Parses and returns the JSON response data containing printer SKU information. This function is used to retrieve printer-specific data for validation and configuration.
functions.usage_example: printer_info = printer_data(); # Returns printer SKU data dictionary

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle or Locator

functions.what_it_does: Extracts CSS styling details from a plan card element. Uses element.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that gets the computed styles using window.getComputedStyle(). Extracts and returns a dictionary containing 'border-radius' and 'margin' CSS properties. This function is used for UI styling validation of plan cards.

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle or Locator

functions.what_it_does: Extracts comprehensive font and text styling details from an element. Uses element.evaluate() to execute JavaScript that gets the computed styles using window.getComputedStyle(). Extracts and returns a dictionary containing: font-family, font-size, font-weight, font-style, line-height, letter-spacing, text-decoration, text-transform, color, font-variant, text-align, background-color, and fill CSS properties. This function is used for detailed typography and styling validation.
functions.usage_example: font_info = get_font_details(text_element); font_family = font_info['font-family']; font_size = font_info['font-size']

parameters.name: element
parameters.description: Playwright ElementHandle or Locator

parameters.name: property
parameters.description: CSS property name to verify

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Expected value for the property

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific CSS property of an element has the expected value. Calls get_font_details() to get all font styling details. Extracts the value of the specified property from the font details dictionary. Asserts that the actual value equals the expected value, raising AssertionError with a descriptive message if not. Returns the actual property value. This function is used for CSS property validation in UI tests.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Font family string to normalize

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes a font-family CSS value by extracting the first font name and removing quotes. Returns the value unchanged if it's empty or None. Splits the value by comma to handle font stacks. Takes the first font name. Removes double quotes and single quotes. Strips whitespace. Returns the normalized font name. This is used for font family comparison where 'Forma DJR UI', sans-serif should match 'Forma DJR UI'.
functions.usage_example: normalized = _normalize_font_family('"Forma DJR UI", sans-serif'); # Returns 'Forma DJR UI'

parameters.name: landing_page
parameters.description: Landing page object with footer element attributes

functions.what_it_does: Validates font styling for all footer elements on the landing page. Defines a list of checks, each containing: attribute name on landing_page object, expected CSS properties dictionary, and label for logging. Iterates through each check. Gets the element from landing_page using getattr(). Asserts the element exists. Calls get_font_details() to get actual font styling. For each expected property, extracts the actual value. If the property is 'font-family', normalizes both actual and expected values using _normalize_font_family(). Asserts actual equals expected, raising AssertionError with descriptive message if not. Logs success for each element. This function performs comprehensive footer typography validation.
functions.usage_example: check_footer_fonts(landing_page); # Validates all footer element fonts

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page object

parameters.name: new_account_auth_option
parameters.description: Authentication option for new account: 'use_password' or 'send_sign_in_code'

functions.what_it_does: Creates a new Instant Ink v2 account with support for new HPID flow variations. Tests browser connectivity with about:blank. Navigates to _instantink_url with domcontentloaded wait. Waits 2 seconds for page to settle. Accepts privacy banner if present. Clicks sign-up button. Clicks create account button. Checks if password screen appears first (sometimes happens). If so, clicks back button to return to email screen. Fills email field with _tenant_email. Clicks next button. Detects if this is a new email flow (firstName field appears) or existing email flow. For new email flow: fills firstName and lastName, validates new_account_auth_option parameter, if 'send_sign_in_code' clicks that button, if 'use_password' clicks use password button and fills password, clicks create button, waits for OTP screen, retrieves and fills verification code, submits OTP, waits for stepper. For existing email flow: clicks use password button, fills password, clicks sign-in button, waits 5 seconds. Navigates to portalshell and waits 60 seconds. Clicks side menu toggle if present. Clicks HP Instant Ink menu item. Returns page object. This is the most comprehensive account creation function supporting multiple HPID flow variations.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML content of verification email

functions.what_it_does: Extracts verification code from email HTML with multiple fallback strategies. Uses BeautifulSoup to parse HTML. First attempts to find paragraph element with class 'code' and extract text. If found, returns it. If not found, extracts all text from the HTML using stripped_strings. Defines multiple regex patterns to match verification codes: 'one-time passcode (OTP): 123456', 'one-time passcode...123456', 'The OTP will expire...123456', and generic 6-digit number. Iterates through patterns attempting to find a match. If a match is found, returns the 6-digit code from the first capture group. If no pattern matches, raises RuntimeError. This is a more robust version of extract_verification_code() that handles various email formats.

classes.class_purpose: Manages multiple browser instances with different profiles and modes (remote debug, incognito, normal). Provides methods to create browsers, switch between them, bring windows to foreground, find windows by URL, wait for new windows, load URLs in specific browsers, and manage browser lifecycle. Maintains a registry of all active browsers with their metadata including driver instances, window handles, tabs, and browser types. Supports OS-specific window management operations for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

classes.docstring:
A flexible test step executor that can handle various action types
    and dynamic step configurations from YAML files.
classes.class_purpose: Core test execution engine that processes YAML test definitions and executes test steps. Manages UI element metadata loaded from JSON files, handles variable substitution in test data, executes various action types including click, set, verify, navigate, scroll, select, and custom actions. Integrates with MultiBrowserManager for multi-browser support, Selenium WebDriver for browser automation, and Azure OpenAI for AI-powered visual verification. Maintains execution logs, handles iframe switching, supports multiple element locator strategies with fallback, and generates CSV reports of test execution results. Provides methods for loading YAML from files or strings, managing control metadata, performing element interactions with retry logic, and tracking test flow execution.

Custom exception class for flow configuration errors. Raised when there are issues with flow setup such as missing flow directories, invalid locale configurations, missing address data, or printer profile resolution failures. Extends the built-in Exception class to provide specific error handling for flow configuration problems.

Manages the complete lifecycle of Playwright browser automation including browser launch, context creation, page initialization, and cleanup. Handles browser type selection (chromium/firefox/webkit) based on target platform configuration. Configures browser context with locale, viewport dimensions, device scale factor, HAR recording, video capture, and proxy settings. Provides context manager interface (__enter__/__exit__) for automatic resource cleanup. Collects and saves HAR logs and video recordings to the run directory when enabled.

Data class representing a single test step in the execution report. Stores all metadata about a step including its source location (file and line number), the expected pattern extracted from source code, execution status (PENDING/PASS/FAIL/NOT_EXECUTED), the actual logged message when executed, timestamp, duration in milliseconds, execution order, number of occurrences, and first timestamp. Used to track both expected steps (discovered via AST scanning) and dynamic steps (discovered during execution).
classes.how_to_instantiate: step = ReportStep(id=1, file='test_flow.py', line=42, pattern='Login successful', status='PENDING')

classes.class_purpose: Custom logging handler that intercepts INFO-level log messages from framework_logger and forwards them to the ReportsManager for step execution tracking. Filters to only process INFO level messages and calls reporter.record_step_execution() with the log message and record. This is the bridge between the logging system and the report generation system.

Main reporting engine that orchestrates the entire test reporting lifecycle. Scans test flow source code using AST parsing to discover expected test steps. Intercepts log messages during test execution to match them against expected steps. Tracks step execution order, timing, and status. Captures stdout, stderr, and log output to a console buffer. Generates comprehensive HTML reports with color-coded step tables, execution statistics, and console logs. Generates companion JSON reports with structured data. Handles dynamic step discovery for steps not found in source code. Provides toggles to enable/disable HTML and JSON report generation.

Inner utility class that implements a tee-like stream wrapper to duplicate output to both the original stream (stdout/stderr) and a StringIO buffer. Used to capture console output while still displaying it to the user. Implements write() and flush() methods to mirror the stream interface.
classes.how_to_import: Not intended for external import - inner class of ReportsManager

classes.inner_class_purpose: Inner utility class that implements a tee-like stream wrapper to duplicate output to both the original stream and a StringIO buffer for console capture

classes.class_purpose: Container class for framework-wide constant values including directory paths, file paths, Excel column headers, color codes for Excel cell formatting, PatternFill objects for cell styling, HTML tags to ignore during scraping, default timeout values, and retry counts. All members are class-level attributes. This class is never instantiated - it serves as a namespace for constants.
classes.how_to_instantiate: Not instantiated - use Constants.ROOT_DIR, Constants.LOG_DIR, etc.

classes.class_purpose: Global state container that holds runtime configuration values set from command-line arguments and loaded from configuration files. Stores values like stack, locale, language, printer_profile, easy_enroll, biz_model, target platform, region, country, country_code, language_code, address_payload, selected_printer_sku, tenant_email, flow_name, timestamp, mode, run_dir, headless flag, payment_method, simulator_platform, collect_logs flag, and requirements dictionary. Provides a reset() class method to clear all state. All members are class-level attributes accessed directly without instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: Not instantiated - use GlobalState.locale, GlobalState.headless, etc.

classes.class_purpose: Configuration initialization class that provides the init() class method to populate GlobalState from command-line arguments and load supporting data from JSON files. The init() method resets GlobalState, sets all provided configuration values, loads locale information from locale_map.json, loads address data from address_data.json, resolves printer SKU from printer_profiles.json, and sets any additional keyword arguments as GlobalState attributes. This class orchestrates the complete framework configuration setup.

Custom dictionary subclass that allows attribute-style access to dictionary keys. Enables accessing dictionary values using dot notation (e.g., elements.username_input) instead of bracket notation (e.g., elements['username_input']). Raises AttributeError if a key does not exist when accessed as an attribute.

Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. Contains methods for interacting with cancellation feedback forms, verifying cancellation timeline UI, validating retention options, handling plan changes during cancellation, and testing various cancellation-related features. All methods are static and operate on page objects passed as parameters.
classes.how_to_instantiate: CancellationPlanHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call CancellationPlanHelper.select_cancellation_feedback_option(page, 'I do not print enough') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Static helper class providing comprehensive utility methods for dashboard and account management testing. Contains methods for dashboard access and navigation, plan verification and management, billing and payment operations supporting multiple payment gateways (PGS, 2CO) and methods (credit card, PayPal, direct debit, Google Pay, Apple Pay), address management, printer selection, savings calculator validation, session handling, and various dashboard feature testing. All methods are static and operate on page objects passed as parameters.
classes.how_to_instantiate: DashboardHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call DashboardHelper.access(page, 'user@test.com') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for email verification operations using Gmail API. Contains methods to check if emails with specific subjects have been received, retrieve email messages by subject, and extract detailed email content including subject, pre-header, and HTML body. All methods are static and do not require class instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: EmailHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call EmailHelper.sees_email_with_subject(email, subject) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Massive helper class containing 100+ static methods that orchestrate the complete HP Instant Ink enrollment workflow.
