Provides methods for every step of enrollment including starting enrollment, signing in, accepting terms, selecting printers and plans, entering shipping and billing information, handling multiple payment methods (credit cards via PGS and 2CO, PayPal, Google Pay, direct debit, prepaid codes), applying promotional codes, validating UI elements and layouts, handling modals, performing enrollment finalization, and validating thank you pages. This is the central orchestration class for all enrollment test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: EnrollmentHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call EnrollmentHelper.start_enrollment(page) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the Fulfillment Service Rails Admin interface. Contains methods to authenticate and access the Rails Admin, search for and navigate to specific orders by trigger ID, validate order status and shipping details, send orders for processing, update order error codes, and perform complete order receive-and-send workflows. All methods are static and leverage the RABaseHelper for common Rails Admin operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: FulfillmentServiceRAHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call FulfillmentServiceRAHelper.access(page) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the Fulfillment Simulator Rails Admin interface. Contains methods to access the simulator Rails Admin, search for and navigate to orders by trigger ID, update order status and tracking information to simulate fulfillment events, and orchestrate complete order processing workflows including triggering background jobs in the Fulfillment Service. All methods are static and leverage RABaseHelper for common Rails Admin operations.
classes.how_to_instantiate: FulfillmentSimulatorRAHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call FulfillmentSimulatorRAHelper.access(page) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Massive helper class containing 60+ static methods for comprehensive Gemini Rails Admin operations. Provides methods for authentication and navigation, tenant and subscription management, billing cycle operations (calculate tally, submit charges, refunds), subscription state transitions, printer management, notification verification, payment credential manipulation, free months management, page tally operations, kit shipment, event shifting for time-based testing, and extensive validation methods. This is the primary interface for backend data manipulation and verification in HP Instant Ink testing.
classes.how_to_instantiate: GeminiRAHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call GeminiRAHelper.access(page) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for HP ID authentication operations. Contains methods for dismissing country selection popups, performing sign-in with credentials, completing full sign-in flows, creating new user accounts with sign-up, verifying account codes via email, and completing full sign-up flows. All methods are static and work with Playwright page objects to automate HPID authentication workflows.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for testing and validating the Instant Ink landing page. Contains methods for opening and accessing the landing page with privacy banner handling, verifying refer-a-friend (RaF) banners and footnotes, validating page sections are in viewport after navigation, testing consumer landing page flows for both desktop (OOBE) and mobile (MOBE) viewports, validating never worry sections, verifying footer hyperlinks, testing cookie settings, validating FAQ links and content, selecting countries, and validating emergency banners with different color schemes. All methods are static and designed to be called without instantiation.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call LandingPageHelper.access(page) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for interacting with the OSS (Online Setup Service) emulator used for printer setup and enrollment testing. Contains methods for navigating to the emulator, signing in with HPID, clicking specific buttons like the 3-month trial button, filling printer configuration details (serial number, SKU, UUID, cloud ID, fingerprint, language, country), selecting app types, starting setup flows, configuring POOBE flows, accepting connected printing services, declining HP Plus, continuing through dynamic security notices and value propositions, selecting printer replacement options, activating HP Plus, selecting countries, verifying reminder checkboxes and trial text, and accessing learn more pages. All methods are static and designed to work with Playwright page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for testing and validating the overview page of the Instant Ink dashboard. Contains methods for verifying cartridge installation messages for different cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), validating multiple cartridge type combinations, checking the number of printers in status-grouped sections, verifying plan suggestion banners for upgrading or downgrading, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status cards with action buttons, testing HP shop ink link navigation, testing enroll printer again functionality, and downloading past invoices. All methods are static and designed for use in test automation scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, 'k') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for testing and validating the print history and payment history page. Contains methods for selecting billing cycle periods, verifying billing table data including pages printed and charges, normalizing and validating currency values across different formats and locales, verifying complete billing sections with multiple data points (pages, rollover, additional pages, prices, charges, tax, totals), validating all billing cycles with expected data sets, testing FAQ sections with link navigation and page verification, validating no-pens print history page state, downloading all invoices, verifying notifications in print history table, and validating plan information and current totals. All methods are static and designed for comprehensive print history testing scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call PrintHistoryHelper.verify_billing_table_data(page, 50) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides a comprehensive set of static utility methods for interacting with the Resque job scheduling system and performing common page navigation and data extraction operations. This helper class is designed to support test automation by providing reusable methods for accessing the Resque admin interface, queuing and monitoring background jobs, navigating to specific pages and menu items, extracting field values and links by title from card-based UI layouts, validating boolean field values, and validating template data with date/time comparisons. All methods are static so no instantiation is required.
classes.how_to_instantiate: RABaseHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load(page, 'PageTitle') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for verifying shipment tracking information and cartridge installation status in the application. Contains methods for validating that specific cartridge colors are displayed as installed, handling multiple cartridge type scenarios with fallback logic, clicking view history actions, and validating shipment history table contents including cartridge counts and welcome kit information. All methods are static and work in conjunction with the ShipmentTrackingPage page object to interact with shipment tracking UI elements.
classes.how_to_instantiate: ShipmentTrackingHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call ShipmentTrackingHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, 'k,c') directly.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for managing subscription plan updates, upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations. Contains methods for verifying the visibility of free trial information, validating that the correct set of plan options is displayed with expected page counts and pricing, verifying which plan is currently selected, selecting plans by page count or position, canceling subscriptions with confirmation handling, validating terms of service link navigation, verifying plan change pending messages, undoing plan changes, and verifying plan information details. All methods are static and work in conjunction with UpdatePlanPage, CancellationPage, and OverviewPage page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: UpdatePlanHelper is used via static methods - no instantiation needed. Call UpdatePlanHelper.select_plan(page, 100) directly.

classes.class_purpose: Empty base class used as a container for element locator definitions. Page object classes define their own ElementLocators inner class that inherits from this base class and adds selector string attributes. This pattern provides a clean namespace for organizing all locators associated with a page. The class itself has no methods or attributes - it serves purely as a structural base for locator storage.
classes.how_to_instantiate: ElementLocators is typically not instantiated directly. Page objects create their own ElementLocators subclass and instantiate it in __init__.

classes.class_purpose: Proxy class that provides convenient wait operations for page elements by dynamically creating wait methods based on element locator names. When a page object accesses self.wait.element_name, this class intercepts the attribute access, retrieves the corresponding selector string from the elements object, and returns a lambda function that calls page.wait_for_selector with that selector. This allows page objects to write clean wait code like self.wait.login_button(timeout=5000) instead of page.wait_for_selector(self.elements.login_button, timeout=5000). Implements the __getattr__ magic method to enable this dynamic behavior.
classes.how_to_instantiate: WaitProxy is instantiated automatically by BasePageObject. Not typically instantiated directly in test code.

classes.class_purpose: Base class for all page objects in the framework, providing core functionality for element locator management and dynamic attribute access. Stores the Playwright Page instance, creates an ElementLocators instance for storing selectors, and creates a WaitProxy instance for wait operations. Implements __getattr__ magic method to enable dynamic attribute access where accessing an attribute name that matches a locator name automatically returns a Playwright locator object for that selector. Provides the get_selector method for retrieving raw selector strings when needed. All page object classes in the framework should inherit from this base class to gain these capabilities.
classes.how_to_instantiate: BasePageObject is typically not instantiated directly. Page object classes inherit from it and call super().__init__(page) in their constructor.

classes.class_purpose: Page object representing the cancellation banner component that displays when a user has scheduled their subscription for cancellation. Provides locators for all interactive elements within the cancellation banner including the link to see the cancellation timeline, the button to keep the subscription, the subscription resume modal with its confirm and back buttons, and the resume confirmation banner that appears after successfully resuming a subscription. This page object is used in test scenarios that verify cancellation banner visibility, subscription resume flows, and cancellation timeline access.

Comprehensive page object that encapsulates all UI elements and interactions across the entire subscription cancellation flow. Covers multiple screens including the cancellation summary page showing subscription details and printer information, the cancellation feedback survey with various reason radio buttons, the change plan modal with all available plan options, pause plan functionality, transfer subscription options, contact support links, cancellation timeline elements, and complete paper subscription cancellation flow with paper-specific feedback options. Provides locators for over 70 distinct UI elements organized by functional area including printer info, cancellation summary, retention alternatives, change plan modal, feedback survey, timeline sections, and paper cancellation elements. Contains a utility method get_plan_button() to dynamically retrieve plan selection buttons based on page count.

Page object representing the cancellation timeline modal that appears after subscription cancellation is confirmed. Encapsulates all UI elements within the timeline modal including the modal container, close button, timeline stepper showing cancellation stages, header icon and text, restore subscription button, retention alternative links (keep subscription, transfer subscription, shop HP), action buttons (preview bill, request recycling), and step icons for visual timeline progression. Provides five utility methods to access timeline elements by index: timeline_stepper_header() for stepper titles, cancellation_step() for step containers with wait logic, cancellation_step_title() for step titles, and third/fourth/fifth_step_icon() for accessing specific step icons. Used in tests to verify timeline display, step progression, and restoration functionality.

Page object representing the Change Plan modal that appears during the cancellation flow as a retention strategy. Encapsulates all UI elements within the change plan interface including the modal container, header title and subtitle, informational icons for plan changes and shipping, plan selection buttons for all available page tiers (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700 pages), additional pages tooltip, upgrade plan section with billing cycle selection (current vs next billing cycle) including radio buttons and icons, navigation buttons (return to cancellation, back, confirm), and visual indicators like dot icons for ink plans. Provides two utility methods: select_ink_plan() to dynamically click a plan button based on page count, and back_button_choose_plan() to access the back button filtered by its text content. Used in cancellation retention tests to verify plan change options and handle plan switching during cancellation flow.

Massive comprehensive page object that encapsulates the entire enrollment confirmation and plan selection flow for HP Instant Ink subscription. Covers multiple enrollment paths including pre-enrollment plan type selection (monthly, yearly, pay-as-you-print), plan selection with ink-only and ink+paper options, shipping address entry and validation with suggested address handling, billing information setup supporting multiple payment methods (credit card via PGS iframe, 2Checkout, PayPal with various authentication flows, Google Pay), promotion and prepaid code application, terms and conditions agreement, automatic renewal notices, paper addon management, and final enrollment confirmation. Contains over 200 locators organized into logical sections: plan selection elements, shipping form fields and validation, billing form fields and payment method selection, PGS iframe elements for credit card entry, PayPal integration elements, Google Pay elements, promotion modal elements, confirmation page elements, OOBE and FTM specific elements, FLIP redesign elements, header navigation elements, and paper subscription elements. Provides two utility methods: select_ink_plan() and select_paper_plan() to dynamically select plans based on page count. This page object supports multiple UI variations and enrollment flows making it the central component for enrollment testing.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the HP Smart Dashboard page. Provides locators for promotional page elements, subscription state indicators, HP Instant Ink enrollment links, printer management options, plan update controls, and footer navigation links. This page object follows the BasePageObject pattern where locators are stored in the self.elements namespace. Used for interacting with the HP Smart dashboard after user authentication.

Encapsulates all UI elements and navigation methods for the dashboard side menu component. Provides locators for all menu items including Instant Ink submenu items (Overview, Update Plan, Print History, Shipment Tracking) and Account submenu items (Shipping & Billing, Notifications). Contains intelligent click methods that automatically handle parent menu expansion for nested menu items and mobile layout menu expansion. Handles responsive design where the side menu may be collapsed in mobile layouts and needs to be expanded before clicking menu items.

Encapsulates UI elements for the Gemini Admin interface. Provides locators for viewing item details in card or definition list format and for accessing the refund payment functionality. This appears to be an internal administrative tool for HP support or operations staff to manage customer subscriptions and process refunds. The minimal set of locators suggests this page object is focused on specific admin tasks rather than comprehensive page coverage.

Encapsulates all UI elements for HP ID authentication pages including sign-in, account creation, and email verification flows. Provides locators for form input fields (first name, last name, email, password), validation helper text elements, submit buttons, country selector, and sign-in options. Covers both the initial sign-in page and the account creation/registration page. Used for automating HP ID authentication flows which are prerequisites for accessing HP Instant Ink services.

Encapsulates all UI elements for the HP Instant Ink public landing page, which is the main marketing and information page for the service. Provides over 80 locators covering every section of the landing page including header navigation (sign in, sign up, country picker, mobile menu), hero section with get started button, how it works section with step descriptions, plan selection section with plan cards and navigation arrows, FAQs with tabs for different plan types, go green section, refer-a-friend section, paper delivery add-on section, footer links, country selector modal, alert banners, and enrollment kit banner. Also provides property methods for accessing specific elements and methods for interacting with plan cards. This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework, reflecting the complexity of the marketing landing page.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Notifications page. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns. Defines locators for the notifications table, table rows, notifications center box, notification icon button, and combined notifications table selector. Provides a property method to access the first matching notifications table element for test automation interactions.

Encapsulates all UI elements and configuration options for the OSS Emulator testing tool. Provides locators for emulator tabs (Dev, Device, App), authentication fields (username, password, sign-in), device configuration fields (serial number, SKU, UUID, cloud ID, fingerprint, registration state), printer setup options (load tray, insert ink, calibration), app type selection, flow control buttons (start flow, continue, next), offer acceptance elements (HP+ activation, decline, consent buttons), and post-OOBE configuration checkboxes. This page object is essential for automated testing of printer enrollment and setup flows in a simulated environment without physical printers.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Instant Ink Overview/Dashboard page. This is the most comprehensive page object in the framework with over 150 locators covering every aspect of the subscription management interface. Provides locators for printer enrollment and replacement flows, status card elements showing printer state and connectivity, monthly print summary with progress bars for different page types (plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, credited pages, additional pages), plan details card showing subscription information and billing/shipping details, ink cartridge status display, special offers and promo code redemption, privacy settings, pause/resume plan functionality, tour tooltips for first-time users, footer navigation links, paper subscription add-on elements, emergency banners, support card, and all modals and interactive elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality and provides several helper methods and properties for accessing frequently used elements.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the version 3 Plan Selector page. Provides locators for the plan selector combobox dropdown, the listbox containing plan options, individual plan option elements, buttons to select ink-only or ink-and-paper plans, the content area displaying plan details, and the continue button to proceed with the selected plan. Also includes header elements like virtual agent link, support phone number, country selector, and account button. Provides a select_plan() method that accepts a plan value (like '10', '50', '100') and dynamically constructs an XPath selector to locate and click the matching plan option in the dropdown. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Print History page. Provides locators for the page title, print history card displaying billing cycle information, progress bars showing plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, and additional pages used, plan details card with billing summary, FAQ card with expandable questions and answers, FAQ links to terms of service and plan overview, print history table with billing cycle data and invoice download links, billing cycle selector dropdown, table elements for date, description, and invoice columns, and paper subscription summary section. Includes property methods for convenient access to the FAQ update plan link and plan price text elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printer Replacement page where users can replace their current printer with a new one. Provides locators for the printer replacement container, setup buttons for configured and non-configured printers, back navigation button, and cards displaying current and new printer information. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the elements attribute.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printer Selection page where users can choose from available printers or add new ones. Provides locators for the printer selection container, printer radio buttons for selection, continue button, add printer card, printer details like images and serial numbers, printer benefits information, carousel navigation arrows, printer offline messages, and connectivity guide links. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the elements attribute.

Encapsulates UI elements and interactions for the Printer Updates page where users can configure automatic update settings for their printers. Provides a locator for the continue button and an action method to accept automatic updates. Sets a custom default timeout of 60 seconds for page operations to accommodate potentially slow update configuration processes. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods.

Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printers management page where users can view and manage their registered printers. Provides locators for printer online status indicators, printer options menu, remove printer functionality, Instant Ink enrollment buttons, printer boxes for enrolled and non-enrolled printers, confirmation dialogs for printer removal, and subscription management buttons. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods through the elements attribute.

Encapsulates UI elements and interactions for the privacy consent banner that appears on the application. Provides a locator for the OneTrust privacy acceptance button and an action method to accept the privacy banner. This is typically used in test setup to dismiss the privacy banner before proceeding with test scenarios. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element access methods.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipment Tracking page where users can view the status of their ink cartridge and paper shipments. Contains locators for different cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), paper tracking items, FAQ accordion sections with questions and answers, shipment history table with date/description/tracking number columns, support card elements, and various help links including recycling and missing ink support. Provides methods to retrieve the page title with configurable wait timeout, access specific FAQ questions by index, and dynamically get selectors by name. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipping and Billing Management page where users can add, edit, and manage their shipping addresses and payment methods. Contains locators organized into three main sections: shipping section (manage address link, form modal, address fields, validation messages, save/cancel buttons), billing section (manage payment link, billing form modal, payment method fields, PayPal logo, credit card input, iframe elements), and page-level elements (page title, notification messages, modal close buttons). Provides a static utility method to select business account type radio button. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and uses the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized locator storage.

Encapsulates UI elements for the application Sign In page. Provides locators for the email/username input field, use password button (likely for switching from other auth methods to password-based auth), password input field, and sign-in submit button. This is a minimal page object focused solely on locator definitions without custom interaction methods, relying on inherited BasePageObject methods for interactions. The import of test_flows_common suggests this page may be used in conjunction with common test flow utilities.

Encapsulates UI elements for the Thank You confirmation page that appears after users complete various processes like enrollment, subscription setup, or printer replacement. Contains locators for the continue button to proceed to the next step, thank you card displaying confirmation content, close modal button for finishing printer replacement flow, email display showing the user's registered email address, cartridge image showing the subscribed cartridge type, and subtitle text with additional confirmation information. This is a locator-only page object without custom interaction methods, relying on inherited BasePageObject functionality.

Encapsulates the UI element for the Terms of Service HP Smart consent page. This is a very minimal page object containing only a single locator for the continue button that users click to accept the terms of service and proceed with the HP Smart application setup or enrollment. The page represents a standard legal consent screen that appears during onboarding or setup flows. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction methods.

Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Update Plan page where users can view their current plan details, change to different page tier plans, upgrade or downgrade plans, pause or resume plans, manage shipping and billing information, redeem prepaid codes, and cancel their Instant Ink subscription. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element management functionality. Contains 60+ locator definitions organized by functional areas and provides dynamic helper methods to access plan elements programmatically.

Named tuple class for storing virtual printer data including entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint. Extends tuple to provide both indexed access and named attribute access to printer information returned by create_virtual_printer() function. Used to pass printer details between printer creation, registration, and claiming functions.
classes.how_to_instantiate: printer = PrinterData(entity_id='ABC123', model_number='1G5L3A', device_uuid='uuid-string', cloud_id='cloud-123', postcard='postcard-data', fingerprint='fingerprint-data')

Toggle button to expand or collapse the side menu in mobile layout. This hamburger menu icon appears in responsive mobile views where the side menu is initially hidden.

Overview menu item under the HP Instant Ink section. Navigates to the subscription overview page showing current plan details and usage.

locators.ui_element_description: Main container element for the printer replacement page. Uses a CSS class selector that matches any class starting with 'printerReplacementPage__printer-replacement-page-container' to identify the root container of the printer replacement interface.

Button element that allows users to proceed with setting up a printer that is already configured. This button appears when the printer has been detected and is ready for setup in the replacement flow.

locators.locator_strategy: data-testid attribute selector with starts-with operator

locators.locator_strategy: CSS descendant selector combining data-testid with input element
locators.ui_element_description: The actual input radio button elements within each printer card. Targets the input elements that are descendants of elements with data-testid starting with 'printer-card'. These are the clickable radio input controls for selecting a printer.

locators.locator_strategy: CSS descendant selector targeting img within printer-image-container class

Element displaying printer details including the serial number and other identifying information. Uses class 'printer-details' to target the container showing printer-specific information like model number and serial number.

locators.locator_strategy: CSS attribute selector with starts-with operator combined with paragraph element

locators.locator_strategy: CSS descendant selector combining data-testid with class selector

locators.locator_strategy: CSS attribute selector with contains operator

locators.locator_strategy: CSS attribute selector with contains operator

locators.locator_strategy: CSS descendant selector combining data-testid with aria-label attribute

The check circle icon visually confirms the printer's online connectivity status.

Printer options menu button or control that opens a menu with various printer management actions. Clicking this element typically reveals options like remove printer, printer settings, or other printer-specific actions.

Button element within the printer options menu that initiates the printer removal process. Clicking this button typically opens a confirmation dialog asking the user to confirm they want to remove the printer from their account.

locators.locator_strategy: CSS attribute selector with starts-with operator combined with :has() pseudo-class

The presence of the printer-supplies element indicates the printer is enrolled and showing supply/ink level information.
